ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: “Oyitsɔ̃ kĩ enyína onyole aatsãmɔ kɔma, ɛma obinyole. Baavɛ ye Imanuel,” kĩ de kayɔ nyɛ, “Baguma deĩ mɔ boe.”

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: ade Azur ma Zadok, Zadok ma Akim, Akim lɛmɛ ma Elihud.

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Ade ayekĩ Yesu Kristo kama fɔlɛtsa a ya nɛ foe kamɛ a nɛ awĩ a. Bitinya ɔma Maria bɛkpa Yosef, lɛmɛ fɔmɔ baapi ye a, Maria mɔ́ eyi ɔsũ kĩ ɛtsɛ̃mɔ kɔma kĩ kuto Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: Ayekĩ Yosef nyɛ oti kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, ade ɛtáwɛ kĩ yado Maria kɔnyɔã nɛ bati anɛ sũ ni, ɛwɛ kĩ yasĩ ye nɛ kabɛla kamɛ.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Kĩ ɛkakɔna fukĩĩ akũ a, boe Ɔlate a ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye nɛ kɔda kamɛ, ebi ye kĩ, “Yosef, Dawid obi, tavila Maria kakɔã, kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kɔma a to.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Yaama obinyole, ote ye dĩ kĩ Yesu, kitonɛkĩ ye aanyɛ ye bati etomɛna tɔkpa kamɛ.”

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Foesũ kĩ Yosef nyinya a, ɛkɔã Maria ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bi ye a.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Lɛmɛ Yosef mɔ Maria táta mɔ akũ ndɛ ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ ɔnyɔ aye, kɛnawo lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛma ye obinyole a. Ade Yosef te ye dĩ kĩ Yesu a.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Bɛma Yesu nɛ Betlehem nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Herodes kanya lɛgã nɛ Yudea tite akũ a. Owi tábuna mɔ banyole badi kĩ bɛkɔlɛ fɔtsa nɛ fusibii kadã akũ to suwa kadekɔ̃ bɛya nɛ Yerusalem,

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: ade bɛyavia kĩ, “Ndia bɛma obisɛ̃ a kĩ yaanya Yudafɔ lɛgã a? Bɔmɔ́ ye lisibii kĩ lide nɛ suwa kadekɔ̃, ade bɔya kĩ bɔ́asum ye a.”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Kĩ Lɛgã Herodes nu fukĩĩ a, ɔwɔlɛ bɔlɛ ye mɔ odi nyɛ odi nɛ Yerusalem a.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: Foesũ ɛvɛ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a flee ɛkpɔta evia be kĩ, “Nɛ ndia baama Kristo a?”

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɛ Betlehem nɛ Yudea tite akũ, kitonɛkĩ ade lɛtsa kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ tsɛlɛ eyi kĩ,

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Foesũ Herodes vɛ anyansãfɔ a kĩ bito suwa kadekɔ̃ a ɛnamɛna kabɛlakɔ̃, ade evia be lɔkɔ paa a kĩ lisibii a nɛ a.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ke liti a, ɛnyɛ be osi kĩ bɛnaa Betlehem mɔ ɔlɛdo kĩ, “Minaawɛ obisɛ̃ a kawɛ wĩ. Ntɛ mimɔ́ ye ni, mifɔ̃ ĩnyi lɔkɔa yĩ malo nnaasum ye.”

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Kĩ biwo nɛ kɔla a kamɛ a, bɛmɔ́ obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma Maria, ade bike aduli bisum ye a. Bibinya be futetsa a kĩ fɔnyɛ sika ngbã, lifii mɔ klubɔũ bɛkpa ye.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Baguma nyɛ ɛtsa be nɛ kɔda kamɛ kĩ bɛtabuki bɛnaa Herodes sɛkɛ̃, foesũ bimuniki bɛtsã osi bɛbã bɛdzakũ bɛnaa ɔtɔ. Yosef Lomɔ Maria Mɔ Yesu Bɛnaa Egipte.

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Kĩ anyansãfɔ a dzakũ a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa Yosef nɛ kɔda kamɛ ebi ye kĩ, “Herodes aatsã ɛwɛ obisɛ̃ a kĩ yakɔ ye, foesũ yidza kakpã obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma milo minaa Egipte tite akũ. Mina nɛ awã kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ maabi nɔ kĩ mimuniki miya.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Lɛnyɛ lɛnyɛ a na, Yosef yidza ɛkpã obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma bilo bɛnaa Egipte tite akũ.

text: Edzi nɛ awã kɛnawo kĩ Herodes ku. Fukĩĩ ya lɔmɔ ayekĩ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ lɛ eyi a fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, “Nvɛ yĩ obi ntomɛna Egipte tite akũ.” Herodes Fɔ̃ Bɛkɔ Kubisɛ̃ɛ̃nyole Nɛ Betlehem.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Kĩ Herodes mɔ́ eyi ɔsũ kĩ anyansãfɔ a kĩ bito suwa kadekɔ̃ a bu ye tɛkɔasia a ni, ɛna kalɔkpa paa. Foesũ edo bati kĩ bɛnaakɔ kubisɛ̃ɛ̃nyole a kĩ kɔtɛ futeli nviã kɛka, kɛyawo akɔkɔa amɛ akũ a flee nɛ Betlehem, mɔ ye tɛmatɔ kĩ tifuã a flee kamɛ. Herodes vɛ futeli nviã kĩ fuketimɔ lɔkɔ a kĩ bibi ye kĩ lisibii a nɛ a.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,

text: Kĩ Herodes ku a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa Yosef nɛ kɔda kamɛ nɛ Egipte,

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ɛnɛɛ ye kɔvɛla nɛ awɛ kĩ yaakɔa ɛwɛ ye kamɔnɛ. Yaafila ye kamɔnɛ eko nɛ oli kamɛ, lɛmɛ yaapila afafa a nɛ fi kĩ fɔádĩ da a kamɛ.” Yohanes Wɔla Yesu Mui.

ref: After Jesus had gone without eating for
text: Kĩ Yesu dzi katanya, lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa fuwi tɛna flee a, kɔlɛ kɔ ye.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,

text: ade lɛtalɛta bɛfɔnɛ ɔkɔlɔ a mɔ be ɔlɛga awɛ biyi, bitomɔ ye a. Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa, Ɛlɛ Baguma Fɔlɛtsa, Ɛtsa Bati Fɔsɔ Lɛmɛ.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Bati kɔdabu baũ kĩ bito Galilea tite akũ, mɔ Tɛmatɔ Ewua kamɛ, Yerusalem ɔmatɔ, Yudea tite akũ mɔ Yordan ɔwɔ liti tite akũ a yatomɔ ye.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Kĩ Yesu mɔ́ bati kɔdabu a, ede ɛnaa ɔtɔ akũ ɛnakɛna akũ edzi. Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yakpɔta nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃.

text: Foesũ mabi mi kĩ míapuli miwo nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a, ntɛ miakɛna nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ kaba mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ a. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Kalɔkpa Kana Akũ.

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,

text: Baguma hila bikĩ be tɔwɔlɛ teĩ nɛ ye odi akũ, kitonɛkĩ ade be aamɔ Baguma a!

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mila ndɛ ɔhaĩ mikpa batidziwa flee. Bɛláapuli bɛkɔla ɔmatɔ kĩ bitsua nɛ ɔtɔ akũ.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Lɛmɛ finyaa mabi mi kĩ mitalɔ fubo ntɛ miakɛna nhihiɛ adi. Mitatani ode, kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛgãkpo yanɛ.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Mitatani tite kitonɛkĩ Baguma abɔa kakekɔ̃ tanɛ ĩye mitani Yerusalem, kitonɛkĩ ɛnyɛ Lɛgã Kpale a ɔmatɔ.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Mila ndɛ lɛdɛmɛ mikpa batidziwa flee. Lɛmɛ ntɛ lɛdɛmɛ yɛla de ɔmɛnɛ ni, osi odima lɛláa kĩ baatsã bibuki bido de ɔmɛnɛ. Lɛdani fɔtsa pupulidza foesũ bata de kawula lɔkɔa bati asumini de.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Odima látɛna ɔsɛdza kakɔa lɔɔda katsa ye, mbom yakɔa ye kate nɛ ɔsɛdza otse akũ nɛ awã kĩ yaakpalɛ fɔkɔ ɛkpa odi nyɛ odi nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Ade Yesu buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mitakɔna kĩ ĩya kĩ mayaafɔnɛ Mose mbla a mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔtsa katsa a awɛ. Ntáya kĩ mayaafɔnɛ foe awɛ, mbom ĩya kĩ mayaakɛna foe fɔkpɔla.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: “Minu kĩ kɔkɔɛ a, bibi bati kĩ, ‘Takɔ oti, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ edi awu ni, baadzɛmɔ ye.’

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Lɛmɛ finyaa mabi mi kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛna kalɔkpa nɛ ɔwaɛ̃ akũ ni, baadzɛmɔ ye. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛvɛ ɔwaɛ̃ kĩ, ‘Ɔkɔasia’ ni, baakpã ye bɛnamɛna nɛ Ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ anɛ bɛdzɛmɔ ye, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛvɛ ɔwaɛ̃ kĩ, ‘ɔyaɛ’ ni, ɛfɛta ɛkpa ode fi luwotu a kamɛ kanaa.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: fɔnɛ nɔ afɔliɛ fɔtsa a awɛ kayi nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a lɔtɔ nɛ awã, kamuniki kanaalɔla mi mɔ ye kayite tɔ̃ fɔmɔ ayakɔa nɔ afɔliɛ fɔtsa ɔkpa Baguma.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Ntɛ odi sama nɔ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ ni, bɔa mbɔdi milɔla mi kayite nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ lideĩ fɔmɔ baavɛ kalɛ a. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye mɔ miwo fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ a, oti a aakɔa nɔ edo nɛ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ awɛ. Fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ a lɛmɛ aakɔa nɔ ɛkpa polisifɔ ade baado nɔ nɛ kɔla a.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ ade awã aana flee kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ ose kɔtɔ a flee oyua a. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Kɔtsɔ̃ Mɔ Kɔnyɔ Liti Kanɛ Akũ.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Bɛlɛ lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaasĩ ɔtsɔ̃ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛkpa ye kɔtsɔ̃ kasĩ ovoli.’

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Minu lɛmɛ kĩ bibi kɔkɔɛ bati a kĩ, ‘Mitatu mi fubo, mbom mikɛna nɛ fubo a kĩ milɔ mikpa boe Ɔlate a akũ.’

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Mikɔ̃ milɛ kĩ, ‘Oo,’ ĩye ‘Oowo.’ Kalɛ bɛbã kĩ mialɛ nɛ kakĩĩ liti to Oti Kpa a sɛkɛ̃. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Tɔkpa Kakɛna Kase Tɔkpa Kɔtɔ Akũ.

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Minu kĩ bɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Linebi nɛ linebi kadokɔ̃, lɔdɔ̃ nɛ lɔdɔ̃ kadokɔ̃.’

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Lɛmɛ finyaa mabi mi kĩ mitakɛna tɔkpa mise tɔkpa kɔtɔ. Ntɛ odi balɛ nɔ awɛ nɛ sɔmɔna ɔlɔtɔ ni, fɔ̃ ɛbalɛ nɔ nɛ sɛba ɔlɔtɔ lɛmɛ.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Ade ntɛ odi sama nɔ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ kĩ yatɛ nɔ atadiɛ anyanglede ni, fɔ̃ ɛtɛ nɔ atadiɛ wulaa a lɛmɛ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ntɛ oti kĩ edeĩ otumi hɛ nɔ kĩ lɔ ye fɔtsa mɔ kuvũ mitsã lisikũ mɔa ni, lɔ foe mitsã fusikũ nviã.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Ntɛ odi wã nɔ litsedi ni, kpa ye, ntɛ odi wɛ kĩ yavɛ litsedi nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ ni, kpa ye ɛvɛ. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Bakesĩnɛ Kalɛ Kawɛ Akũ.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Minu kĩ bɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Wɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ, kakesĩ nɔ bakesĩnɛ.’

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Lɛmɛ finyaa mabi mi kĩ miwɛ mi bakesĩnɛ kalɛ ade mibɔa mpaɛ nɛ bikĩ bitomɔ mi liti a lɔlɔ,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: ayekĩ míadani boe Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a babi. Kitonɛkĩ ye afɔ̃ suwa ata kakpa bati wĩ mɔ bati kpa flee, ade yafɔ̃ oni ako kakpa bati kĩ badã kpĩĩ mɔ bikĩ baádã kpĩĩ a flee a.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Ntɛ miawɛ bikĩ bawɛ mi kalɛ odi a, kɔtɔkase kɔɔdabe míana? Tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ malo akɛna foe lɔmɔ!

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ade ntɛ mi bakɔba odi miado onukpɛ̃ ni, litsa mitɛnɛ kakɛna? Amamufɔ malo akɛna foe lɔmɔ!

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Foesũ midã kĩ mikpɔla ndɛ ayekĩ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a lɛmɛ kpɔla a.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Midã kadã wĩ kĩ miláakɛna mi ɔsum fɔtsa a nɛ kanade anɛ ayekĩ bati aamɔ lɛtsa a kĩ miakɛna a. Ntɛ mikɛna foe nɛ bati anɛ ni, miláana kɔtɔkase mitomɛna mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a sɛkɛ̃.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Foesũ nɔ kɔ̃ ntɛ akɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ ɔkpa ohiãni ni, tafɔ̃ nɔ ɔkɔba paa malo linyi lɛtsama nɛ foe akũ.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Ntɛ miabɔa mpaɛ ni, mitakɛna ndɛ anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ a aye. Bawɛ kayidza kadza kabɔa mpaɛ nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ mɔ tɔblɔnu fɔbalikɔ̃ ayekĩ odi nyɛ odi aamɔ be. Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ bɛtɛ be kɔtɔkase a lodo kɔkɔɛ.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Lɛmɛ ntɛ abɔa mpaɛ ni, wo nɛ nɔ kɔla kamɛ, kabalɛ kɔla a lɔkɔa kabɔa mpaɛ kakpa nɔ Ɔlɛga a kĩ bɔámɔ mɔ anɛ a, lɔkɔa Aga a kĩ yamɔ fɔtsa a kĩ akɛna nɛ kabɛla kamɛ a aase nɔ kɔtɔ.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Ntɛ miabɔa mpaɛ ni, mitalɛ fɔlɛtsa beblebee kĩ fɔlá kayɔ ndɛ ayekĩ amamufɔ akɛna a. Be bakɔna kĩ ntɛ be mpaɛbɔ deĩ lugo a mɔ Baguma aatse be.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Mitakɛna mi akũ ndɛ be, kitonɛkĩ mi Ɔlɛga nyina lɛtsa a kĩ lisĩ mi kɔkɔɛ fɔmɔ miavia ye malo.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Foesũ mibɔa mpaɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlɛga kĩ edeĩ nɛ ode, nɔ dĩ akũ lɛkpalɛ.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Nɔ lɛgãkanya lɛya. Bɛkɛna nɔ kawɛ nɛ tite akũ, ndɛ ayekĩ bakɛna nɛ ode a.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ mikɔa bati tɔkpa a kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ mi akũ mite be ni, mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a lɛmɛ aakɔa mi tide a ete mi.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Lɛmɛ ntɛ mitákɔa bati tɔkpa mite be ni, mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a lɛmɛ lɛláakɔa mi tɔkpa a ete mi. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Onukpɛ̃ Kasui Akũ.

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Ntɛ asui onukpɛ̃ ni, vɔlɛ anɛ, do kugua nɛ lɔlɔ kamɛ kasɛla tɔlɔ,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: ayekĩ bati bɛbã lɛláanyi kĩ asui onukpɛ̃. Nɔ Ɔlɛga a, kĩ baámɔ mɔ anɛ a odi aanyi, ade Ɔlɛga a kĩ yamɔ fɔtsa nɛ kabɛla kamɛ a aase nɔ foe kɔtɔ a. Ode Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ Nyɛ Akũ Kana Fɔtsa Kakpɔtakɔ̃.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Mifɔ̃ anɛ ɛyɔɔlɛ mi ɛba nɛ Baguma ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a liti katomɛna mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ ɛwɛ etomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃ kĩ mikɛna a akũ, lɔkɔa yaakɔa fɔtsa bɛbã kĩĩ flee ebu ɛkpa mi.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Foesũ mitaha akũ nɛ kunya akũ, kunya luwi a aana de mɔawɛ ɔhau. Luwi nyɛ luwi kamɛ ɔhau pɔnɛ ɛkpa de.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Mitawɛ akũ kana fɔtsa miyi akũ nɛ tite kĩĩ akũ, awã kĩ fupuka mɔ nkandɔlɔ aabula foe kade, ade bayekpanɛ lɛmɛ abubuli kɔla kawo kaye foe a.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Mbom miwɛ akũ kana fɔtsa miyi akũ nɛ ode, awã kĩ fupuka mɔ nkandɔlɔ lɛláabula foe kade, ade bayekpanɛ lɛmɛ lɛláapuli bubuli biwo biye foe a.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Kitonɛkĩ awã kĩ nɔ fɔtsa mɔ kɔya deĩ ni, ade awã nɔ ɔwɔlɛ lɛmɛ deĩ a. Oti Anɛ Nyɛ Ye Ɔsɛdza.

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Lɛmɛ ntɛ nɔ banebi lɛnɛ́nɛ ni, nɔ oti mɔawɛ a aana nɛ ɔtũ kamɛ. Foesũ ntɛ ɔhaĩ a kĩ edeĩ nɛ nɔ kamɛ a nyɛ ɔtũ ni, nɔ oti mɔawɛ kamɛ aake ɔtũ paa. Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Nɛ Baguma Liti Katomɛna Mɔ Akũ Kana Fɔtsa Liti Katomɛna Akũ.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Odima lɛláapuli esum balate nviã, kitonɛkĩ yaakesĩ ɔmɔa lɔkɔa ɛwɛ ɔyade a kalɛ, ĩye yaakɔa akũ ɛmɛnamɔ ɔmɔa esupa ɔyade a. Ɔnɔkɔali nyɛ kĩ ɔláapuli osum Baguma mɔ katinya kamɛ fɔtsa.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: “Foesũ mitakaha akũ mikalɛ kĩ, ‘Litsa bɔ́anya, litsa bɔ́amɔa ĩye litsa bɔ́asua akũ?’

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Ade foesũ mabi mi kĩ mitaha akũ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ míanya, mimɔa midzi nkpã akũ, ĩye tilima a kĩ míasua akũ ĩye midzi a akũ. Mbɔɛɛ nkpã tátɛ kɔya ɛba funitsã, ĩye mbɔɛɛ oti mɔawɛ a tátɛ kɔya ɛba tilima mɔ tɛtadiɛ?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Midã kɔvɔlabi, kɔádu funitsã, kɔákpɔta funitsã kako nɛ tili kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a adɔɔla koe! Mbɔɛɛ mitátɛ kɔya miba kɔvɔlabi?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Lɔkɔa nde sũ miaha akũ nɛ tilima mɔ tɛtadiɛ akũ? Midã ayekĩ lupo kamɛ futse ako nhuilɛ kabo anɛ, fɔákɛna adzuma, fɔátaɛ tileti kanɔ tilima kakpa akũ.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ Lɛgã Salomo mɔ ye akũ kana fɔtsa a flee malo táhɛhɛ fɔlɛ kadã ndɛ nhuilɛ kĩĩ kamɛ amɔa.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ntɛ Baguma kɛɛlɛ lupo kamɛ tuwĩ lɔmɔ, kĩ tideĩ nkpã nviã, ade kunya tiku, bipila te nɛ luwotu kamɛ a ni, ɛláakɛɛlɛ mi ɛba te? Mi katɛkado adã kabii paa!

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Ade fukĩĩ bamɛ liti amamufɔ atomɛna a. Mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a nyina kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ flee sĩ mi.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Mitadzɛmɔ bati bɛbã fɔfɔ̃ Baguma lɛmɛ lɛdzɛmɔ mi,

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: kitonɛkĩ ayekĩ midzɛmɔ bati bɛbã a, ade aye Baguma lɛmɛ aadzɛmɔ mi a, ade mbla a kĩ mikɔa midzɛmɔ bati bɛbã a, ye mɔapɛ a yakɔa ɛdzɛmɔ mi malo a.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Nde sũ adã sukuluku kĩ sideĩ nɛ awaɛ̃ linebi kamɛ lɛmɛ aámɔ luvokũ a kĩ lideĩ nɛ nɔ mɔawɛ linebi kamɛ a?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Nde aapuli obi awaɛ̃ kĩ, ‘Make nɔ aduli, fɔ̃ ĩnyɛ sukuluku nɛ nɔ linebi kamɛ’ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ luvokũ deĩ nɛ nɔ mɔawɛ linebi kamɛ a?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Nɔ anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa ɔkɛnanɛ! Nya lɔtɔ kanyɛ luvokũ a nɛ nɔ linebi kamɛ lɔkɔa aapuli ɔmɔ́ yededeede ɔnyɛ sukuluku nɛ awaɛ̃ linebi kamɛ a.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ntɛ mi a kĩ minyɛ bati kpa a, minyina ayekĩ miate mi babi fɔtsa wĩ a ni, titi eni mɔ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode lɛláakɔa ye fɔtsa wĩ pii ɛkpa bikĩ bavia ye a?

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: “Mi kamɛ obilɛga ɔɔdabe deĩ kĩ ntɛ obi via ye abodoo ni, yaabɔɛ de edo nɛ ye awɛ?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Mivia fɔtsa lɔkɔa baakpa mi, miwɛ fɔtsa lɔkɔa míamɔ foe, mibɔa opunu lɔkɔa baabinya bɛkpa mi.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛwã fɔtsa ni, yana foe. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛwɛ litsedi ni, yaamɔ de, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ yabɔa opunu ni, baabinya bɛkpa ye.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ĩye ntɛ evia ye kafɔabi kĩ yaakɔa kɔɛ ɛkpa ye?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Foesũ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ miawɛ kĩ bati bɛbã bɛkɛna bɛkpa mi ni, mi malo mikɛna foe lɔmɔ mikpa be. Ade fukĩĩ Mose mbla a mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa a atsa a. Mitsã Onyukpɛ̃ Manɛwa a Kamɛ Miwo.

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “Foesũ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yatse yĩ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ mɔ ɛkɛna nɛ foe akũ ni, ɛla ndɛ ɔnyansãni kĩ eke ye kɔla nɛ de mɔ ɔsĩ akũ.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Lɛmɛ onyukpɛ̃ a kĩ yanaa nkpã kamɛ a manɛ, ade osi a kĩ ɛwaa ɛnaa a awã akũ katsã deĩ ɔsĩ, ade bati sukuloo lɛmɔ ye a. Kutse Nyɛ Kutse Mɔ Koe Babi Kĩ Kɔawũ.

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Midã mi akũ kadã wĩ nɛ fɔvã tunukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ akũ. Baya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ miamɔ ndɛ baveli aye, lɛmɛ nɛ be kamɛkamɛ ni, bɛla ndɛ kulikii kĩ kude adɔ̃.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Míakɔa be fɔkɛnatsa minyi be. Ɔgɔabi láwũ akutu, ade ɔtatsuɛ láwũ asãã babi a.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Kutse wĩ awũ babi wĩ, ade kutse kpa lɛmɛ awũ babi kpa a.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Kutse wĩ lɛláapuli kuwũ babi kpa, ade ayea kutse kpa lɛmɛ lɛláapuli kuwũ babi wĩ a.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Lɛmɛ kutse nyɛ kutse kĩ kɔáwũ babi wĩ ni, batsua koe kata kado nɛ fi kamɛ.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Foesũ fɔvã onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a fɔkɛlatsa míakɔa minyi be. Owei Aanaa Ode Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a Kamɛ?

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yatse yĩ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ mɔ yaákɛna nɛ foe akũ ni, ɛla ndɛ odzimbifɔ kĩ eke ye kɔla nɛ dã kamɛ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Oni kpale ko, tɔwɔ yili ade ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ vũ ɛyabɔa kɔla ade kuveli, kɔtɔ gbloo, kububuli a!” Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Mɔ Otumi.

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “Nnɛ́ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yavɛ yĩ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate! Boe Ɔlate!’ aanaa ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ, mbɔɛntɛ bikĩ bakɛna nɛ Nda kawɛ akũ a odi aanaa awã.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Ntɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a wo ɔtɔ a, bati pii aavɛ yĩ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate! Boe Ɔlate! Nnɛ́ nɔ dĩ akũ bɔlɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa, bɔblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa butomɛna bati kamɛ, ade bɔkɛna awãwã fɔtsa pii lɛmɛ a?’

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Ade awã mɔ maabi be kĩ, ‘Ĩnyína mi koũ. Midzakũ nɛ yĩ anɛ kamɛ, mi tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ!’ Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Fɔla Bakenɛ Nviã Akũ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Oni kpale ko, ade tɔwɔ yili, ɔwɛ vũ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ɛyabɔa kɔla a, lɛmɛ kɔtáveli, kitonɛkĩ bike koe nɛ de mɔ ɔsĩ akũ.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Kĩ Yesu yua fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kalɛ a, akũ dzuli bati kɔdabu a nɛ ye fɔtsa katsa a akũ.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Kitonɛkĩ ɛtsa fɔtsa ndɛ oti kĩ edeĩ otumi, kĩ fɔtána ndɛ mbla batsanɛ a fude.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Lɛmɛ Baguma aata bikĩ be mbom la lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a ewula nɛ ɔtũ kamɛ, awã kĩ baabe, bɛba adɔ̃.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Kĩ olobe wo ɔtɔ a, bati kpã bikĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ be kamɛ a bɛma Yesu. Ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a etomɛna be kamɛ mɔ kalɛ mɔapɛ, ade ɛtsa basɔnɛ a flee lɛmɛ a.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Ade awã mɔ onyole odi kĩ yasɔ fɔsɔ ngbã, kĩ bati avila a, yake aduli nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ, ebi ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, ntɛ fɔnyɛ nɔ kawɛ ni, aapuli ɔfɔ̃ fɔsɔ kĩĩ lɛwɔ nɛ yĩ akũ.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Yesu yo awɛ ɛta ye ade ebi ye kĩ, “Mawɛ lɔmɔ. Nɔ akũ ɛkpalɛ.” Ade lɛtalɛta fɔsɔ a wɔ nɛ ye akũ a.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Maaya ĩyaatsa ye.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Kĩ Yesu nu fukĩĩ a, fɔkɛna ye wãwã, ade ebi bati a kĩ bitomɔ ye a kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali mabi mi kĩ ntámɔ odima nɛ Israel kĩ edeĩ katɛkado kĩĩ odu.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: “Boe Ɔlate, yĩ ɔsumunɛ waa nɛ kɔla yasɔ, yaápuli ekuna akũ, ade ɛlamɔ lubo kamɔ paa a.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Mabi mi pipiipi kĩ bati pii aato suwa kadekɔ̃ mɔ suwa katomukɔ̃ bɛyaadzi nɛ opunu akũ mɔ Abraham, mɔ Isak mɔ Yakob nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Ke liti a, Yesu bi sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ, “Naa kɔla! Fɔ́aya nɛ foe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ ɔtɛ odo a.” Ade ye ɔsumunɛ a saɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a na pɛpɛɛpɛ kamɛ a. Yesu Tsa Bati Pii Fɔsɔ.

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ade lɛtalɛta bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Nɔ Baguma Obi, litsa deĩ nɛ bɔmɔ nɔ kayite? Kaya ɔya kĩ ayaakɛna boe tɛnya fɔmɔ lɔkɔ wĩ a aawo ɔtɔ mbɔɛɛ?”

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Foesũ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bati flee nɛ́ kĩ banaadã Yesu, ade kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye a, bike ye aduli kĩ ɛdzakũ nɛ be tite akũ ɛnaa.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Kĩ Yesu naa Petro tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ɛmɔ kĩ Petro nyina waa nɛ ɔkɛna akũ yasɔ atilili.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Yesu pi ye nɛ kɔkpɔ ade lɛtalɛta atilili a dɔ ye, eyidza ete opunu ɛkpa ye a.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Fukĩ ɛkɛna kĩĩ ya nɛ foe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya lɛ eyi kĩ, “Ye mɔawɛ lɛkɔa boe lubo kamɔ, mɔ fɔsɔ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ ete nɛ ye akũ!” Bati Kĩ Bɛwɛ Kĩ Baatomɔ Yesu.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Ade mbla batsanɛ a kamɛ odi ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ɛyabi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, maatomɔ nɔ nnaa kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ aanaa.”

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa lɛmɛ bi ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, maatomɔ nɔ, lɛmɛ fɔ̃ nnaabila nda tɔ̃.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nadzɛla ye, ade bibi ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, tɛ boe o-o! Buku buyua!”

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yesu wo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ odi kamɛ ade ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a tomɔ ye a.

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Lɛtalɛta ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ odi do kavũ awɛ nɛ ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a akũ, kabɔa mui kako nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ, kĩ fɔfɔ̃ kite sukuloo ɔkɔlɔ a adoomu, lɛmɛ Yesu kɔ̃ waa tida.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Odi nyɛ odi kɛna saa, ade bɛkavia akũ kĩ, “Oti ɔdabe odu nyɛ okĩĩ? Ɔwɛ mɔ mui malo atse ye ɔlɛdo!” Yesu Tsa Banyole Nviã Badi Kĩ Hũhũ Kpa Deĩ Nɛ Be Kamɛ.

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Kĩ Yesu nawo Gadara tite akũ nɛ ɔwɔ lutubu baũ liti a, bati nviã kĩ hũhũ kpa na nɛ be kamɛ nɛ́ bito fɔkɛ kĩ babila banɔkpa kamɛ bɛyatsɛlamɔ ye. Ayekĩ be akũ deĩ libe sũ ni, odima lapuli katsã awã osi a katɛ̃.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Awã fuã kakɔ a kĩ baplakuo kɔdabu baũ kudi dza kɔkadɛ a.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Hũhũ kpa a ke Yesu aduli kĩ, “Ntɛ aablɔmɛna boe ni, fɔ̃ bɔnaawo nɛ baplakuo kɔdabu nɛ akĩĩ a kamɛ.”

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Minɛ minaa.” Foesũ bɛnɛ bɛnawo nɛ baplakuo a kamɛ. Ade baplakuo kɔdabu a flee wula atsɛ kliklikli bɛka lɔwɔtuni geĩ a biwo nɛ lutubu a kamɛ, bɛmɔa mui biku a.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Bayaɛ akũ badãmɛnanɛ a lo bɛnaa ɔmatɔ kamɛ bɛnalɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔya a flee nɛ banyole nviã a kĩ hũhũ kpa na nɛ be kamɛ a akũ.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Yesu de ewo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ emuniki ɛtsã mui a anɛ ɛnaa ye awɛ ɔmatɔ kamɛ.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yesu nu lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkalɛ a, ade ɛkpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Basɔnɛ lisĩ bɛkpa tutsonu ɔkɛnanɛ, nnɛ́ bikĩ bideĩ akũ ɔsĩ.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: “Ade ayea odima láko waĩ vɔɛ̃ nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ kɔɛ kamɛ a, kitonɛkĩ ɔyaɛ kuvũ a aayili kɔbɛ̃, waĩ a liwula, ade ɔyaɛ kuvũ a lɛmɛ aabu kade a. Mbom bako waĩ vɔɛ̃ nɛ ɔyaɛ kuvũ vɔɛ̃ kamɛ lɔkɔa foe atsu nviã a flee lɛláabu kade.” Yesu Tsa Otsole Odi Mɔ Yairo Obitsole.

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ gba nɛ tite a na akũ flee. Yesu Tsa Banɛkunɛ Nviã Badi.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Adeke Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ badi bi be akũ kĩ, “Onyole kĩĩ alɛ fubusuotsa!”

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Kĩ Yesu mɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkakɔna a, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nde sũ miakɔna tɔkpa nɛ mi tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ?

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: Lɛmɛ kĩ míanyi kĩ yĩ Otidziwa Obi, ndeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ makɔa bati titikpa kate nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ sũ ni, midã o-o!” Foesũ ebi oti a kĩ eveliveli a kĩ, “Yidza kadza, bɔɛ nɔ ɔkɛna kanaa nɔ awã!”

ref: The man got up and went home.
text: Onyole a yidza ɛdza, ɛdzakũ ɛnaa ye awã.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: Minaawɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kalɛ kĩĩ kayɔ, ‘Sɛmɛ kalɛ kakɛna mawɛ, nnɛ́ bayaɛ afɔliɛ kabɔa mawɛ.’ Ntáya kĩ mayaavɛ bikĩ badã kpĩĩ, mbom ĩya kĩ maavɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ.” Bivia Yesu Fɔlɛtsa Nɛ Onukpɛ̃ Kasui Akũ.

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Kĩ Yesu to awã yanaa a, banɛkunɛ nviã badi tomɔ ye nɛ liti bɛkabɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Dawid obi, mɔ boe kɔnya!”

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Kĩ banyole a nɛ banaa a, bati badi kpã oti bɛbã kĩ yaápuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla, kitonɛkĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ a bɛma Yesu.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã, ade kĩ ɛtsɛ̃ yanaa a, ɛmɔ́ ɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ odi kĩ bavɛ Mateo kĩ edzi nɛ ye ɔgɔlɛ katekɔ̃. Ebi ye kĩ, “Tomɔ yĩ.” Ade Mateo yidza etomɔ Yesu a.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Kĩ Yesu kanya nɛ Mateo awã a, tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ pii mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ bɛbã yadzi nɛ opunu akũ, bibumɔ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛnya.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
text: Farisifɔ a badi mɔ́ foe ade bivia ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Nde sũ mianya kamɔa mɔ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ?”

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Kĩ Yesu kalɛ kɔdzɛla a, Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, ade eke aduli nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ, ebi ye kĩ, “Yĩ obitsole ku finyaakĩĩ, lɛmɛ yaate awɛ nɛ ye akũ ayekĩ yaanyinya.”

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Kĩ Yesu dani ɛmɔ́ otsole a, ebi ye kĩ, “Yĩ obi, nɔ ɔwɔlɛ ɛka edzi. Nɔ katɛkado tsa nɔ.” Otsole a akũ de ɔsĩ nɛ lɔkɔ a na pɛpɛɛpɛ kamɛ.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Kĩ Yesu sila ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a nɛ ye kamɛ a, epuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla. Fɔkɛna bati kɔdabu a wãwã, ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɔtámɔ tɔ̃ fukĩĩ odu nɛ Israel.”

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Ke liti a, Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ade bivia ye kĩ, “Nde sũ bɔmɔ Farisifɔ bɔawɛ onukpɛ̃ kasui, lɛmɛ nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kɔ̃ lásui onukpɛ̃ koũ?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kakɔna miakɔna kĩ obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ bakɔba aadzi nɛ ayɔlɔhɔ kamɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ edeĩ nɛ be kamɛ a? Fɔláanya lɔmɔ. Lɛmɛ luwi a aaya kĩ baakpã obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ a bɛdzakũmɛna nɛ be sɛkɛ̃, ade lɔkɔ a na kamɛ mɔ́ baasui onukpɛ̃ a.

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Odima lákɔa limakũ vɔɛ̃ kataɛ atadiɛ kɔɛ, kitonɛkĩ limakũ vɔɛ̃ a aavɛlɛ lɔkɔɛ a lɛbɛ̃, lɔkɔa ɔkɛ baũ aawa ɛba ɔkɔɛ a.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ɛlɛ nɛ ye lɔlɔ kamɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ nsila nta ye atadiɛ odi pɛ, maasaɛ.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Ade Yesu yidza bɛmɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bitomɔ ye bɛnaa a.

ref: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
text: Otsole odi kĩ babɔanɛ kawula ye futeli ewua-nviã flee natsã Yesu liti, ɛta ye atadiɛ sɔtɔbi.

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Ke liti a, Yesu tomɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a biwo nɛ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ. Kĩ ɛmɔ́ tukutɔnɔ basãnɛ mɔ bati beblebee a kĩ bɛlamɛna kabe yoo a,

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: ebi be kĩ, “Mi flee minɛ, kabisɛ̃ɛ̃tsole a táku, mbom tida kɛwaa!” Ade bati a flee mɛmɛ ye a.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Kĩ bɛblɔmɛna bati a bɛnɛmɛna a, Yesu wo nɛ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a sɛkɛ̃ nɛ kɔla a kamɛ. Epi ke nɛ kɔkpɔ ade kinyinya kiyidza kɛdza a.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Kĩ Yesu wo nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ a, banɛkunɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, ade evia be kĩ, “Mitɛ mido kĩ maapuli ntsa mi?” Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, boe Ɔlate!”

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: Foesũ Yesu kɔa awɛ ɛta be banebi ɛlɛ kĩ, “Fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ mitɛ mido a.”

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Ade be anɛ binya bɛmɔ́ fɔkɔ a. Yesu si be mbla mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ, “Mitabi odima fukĩĩ!”

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Lɛmɛ bɛdzakũ nɛ awã bɛnagbamɛna fɔlɛtsa a nɛ Yesu akũ nɛ tite a amantam a flee kamɛ. Yesu Tsa Omumu Odi.

ref: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
text: Lɛmɛ Farisifɔ a lɛ kĩ, “Hũhũ kpa a lɛgã lɛkpa ye kɔbɛ̃ ɛnɛɛ yablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa.” Bati Kɔdabu a Akũ Kɔ́ Yesu Kɔnya.

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yesu mini nɛ tɛmatɔ mɔ tukula a kamɛ. Ɛtsa fɔtsa nɛ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a, ɛlɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ ode lɛgã kanyakɔ̃ a akũ, ade ɛtsa fɔsɔ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ lɛmɛ a.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Kĩ ɛmɔ́ bati kɔdabu a, be akũ kɔ ye kɔnya, kitonɛkĩ kipiã be, bɛlá obumɛnanɛ ade bɛgba biko ndɛ baveli kĩ bɛlá ɔkpãnɛ a.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Foesũ ebi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Fudutsa a kĩ baaveli a pɔnɛ, lɛmɛ adzuma bakɛnanɛ a lɛpɔ́nɛ.

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Foesũ mike kɔba ɔlate a kĩ ye fudutsa baaveli a aduli, kĩ edo adzuma bakɛnanɛ bɛyaakpɔta foe.”

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: Filipo mɔ Bartolomeo mɔ Toma, Mateo ɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ a, mɔ Alfeo obi Yakobo mɔ Tadeo,

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Ntɛ bɛkpã mi bɛnamɛna fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ ni, mitaha akũ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ míalɛ mɔ ayekĩ míadzɛla a akũ. Ntɛ lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ a, Baguma aakpa mi fɔlɛtsa a kĩ míalɛ a,

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Mbom minaa Israelfɔ a kĩ bɛla ndɛ baveli kĩ bɛyɛla osi a sɛkɛ̃.

ref: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
text: “Midã, mado mi ndɛ baveli aye nɛ kulikii kamɛ, foesũ minyi kadi ndɛ bɔɛ aye, midɔla akũ bɔɛɛ ndɛ mblɔduma aye.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: Simon kĩ epili ye kade lɔlɔ, mɔ Yuda Iskariot kĩ ye lɛnyɛ Yesu ɛkpa a. Yesu Do Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ Ewua-nviã a Fɔtɔ.

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Yesu do bati ewua-nviã kĩĩ fɔtɔ mɔ ɔlɛdo kĩ, “Mitanaa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a tite akũ ĩye miwo nɛ Samariafɔ tɛmatɔ kamɛ.

ref: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
text: Odima ɛtabɔɛ ositsãnɛ kuvũ, atadiɛ ayade, mpabua ayade ĩye otse kĩ bake katsã ɛlɛ nɛ osi a akũ. Kɛfɛta kĩ bɛkpa adzuma ɔkɛnanɛ lɛtsa a kĩ esĩ a.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Ntɛ miwo ɔmatɔ ĩye akula adi kamɛ ni, miwɛ oti odi kĩ yaatunɔ ɛtɛ mi, lɔkɔa mise nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, mina nɛ awã kɛnaawo kĩ míadzakũ.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Mabi mi ɔnɔkɔali a kĩ nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ a, Baguma aamɔ Sodom mɔ Gomora bati kɔnya ɛba ɔmatɔ a na kamɛ bati. Yesu Bi Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Lubokamɔ a Kĩ Baamɔ a.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Bati aakɔa be mɔawɛ bawaɛ̃ bɛkpa kĩ bɛkɔ, ade balɛga lɛmɛ aakɛna foe lɔmɔ nɛ be mɔawɛ babi akũ, ade babi aayidza bɛdza nɛ be bamanɛ akũ bɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔ be a.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Midã mi akũ kadã wĩ, kitonɛkĩ bati aakɔa mi bido nɛ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ awɛ, ade baabo mi nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Ade nɛ yĩ sũ baakpã mi bɛnamɛna ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ fɔgã anɛ, lɔkɔa fɔnya adansiɛ kĩ míadi nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ mikpa be mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ mi aalɛ kɔdzɛla, mbom mi Ɔlɛga Hũhũ a aalɛ kɔdzɛla ɛtsãmɛna mi akũ.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Bati flee aakesĩ mi nɛ yĩ sũ, lɛmɛ oti a kĩ yaadza pintii kɛnaawo kawũna ni, Baguma aakpa ye nkpã.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Ntɛ bitomɔ mi liti nɛ ɔmatɔ odi kamɛ ni, milo minaa ɔbɛbã kamɛ. Mawɛ kĩ minyi kĩ miláayua adzuma kakɛna nɛ Israel tɛmatɔ a kamɛ fɔmɔ Otidziwa Obi a aaya.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ odima lɛbɛ́ ye fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ, ade ɔsumunɛ lɛmɛ lɛbɛ́ ye ɔlate a.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Kɛnɛ kĩ fuwĩ ɔkɔlɛnɛ kĩ yayaakɛna ndɛ ye fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ, ade ɔsumunɛ lɛmɛ kĩ yayaakɛna ndɛ ye ɔlate a. Ntɛ bɛvɛ kɔla ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi kĩ hũhũ kpa flee lɛgã a, buĩ kpa badabe bɛláavɛ kɔla babi? Oti a Kĩ Kɛnɛ Kĩ Bivila.

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Mitavila bati a kĩ bakɔ oti sukɛna lɛmɛ bɛláapuli bɛkɔ ɔkla a, mbom mivila Baguma a kĩ yaapuli ɛwɔla oti sukɛna mɔ ye ɔkla flee nɛ fi luwotu kamɛ a.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Kaplɛ mɔapɛ miakɔa kaya funitataɛ nviã, lɛmɛ koe kamɛ kamɔapɛ malo látɔ nɛ tite kĩ fɔnyɛ́ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a kawɛ.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Mi kɔ̃ Baguma kã mi lɔlɔ tɔlɔ malo flee.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Foesũ mitavila, kitonɛkĩ mitɛ kɔya miba funitataɛ beblebee. Yesu Kamɛ Kabinya Mɔ Ye Kasĩ.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaabɔa yĩ dĩ nɛ bati anɛ kĩ ye ode manɛ ni, yĩ malo maakɛna foe lɔmɔ nkpa ye nɛ Nda nɛ ode a anɛ.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi sĩ yĩ nɛ bati anɛ ni, yĩ malo maasĩ ye nɛ Nda nɛ ode a anɛ. Bati Kayite Aasɛ̃ Nɛ Yĩ Sũ.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Mitakɔna kĩ lukudɔ nkɔa ĩyamɛna nɛ katinya kamɛ. Oowo, ntáya mɔ lukudɔ, mbom kɔpã nkɔa ĩyamɛna.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: Ĩya kĩ makɛna ‘babinyole biyidza bɛdza nɛ balɛga akũ, babitsole liyidza bɛdza nɛ bama akũ, babi batsɔ̃ biyidza bɛdza nɛ be nyina amɛ akũ.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Oti bakesĩnɛ aanya ye mɔawɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ bati.’

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ ɔlɛga ĩye ɔma kalɛ kaba yĩ ni, ɛtákpɔla kĩ yanya yĩ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ, ade ayea odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ ye obinyole ĩye obitsole kalɛ kaba yĩ ni, ɛtákpɔla kĩ yanya yĩ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtábɔɛ ye asendua etomɔ yĩ nɛ liti ni, ɛtákpɔla kĩ yanya yĩ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛwɛ kĩ yapili ye nkpã ni, yaayɛla ye, lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaayɛla ye nkpã nɛ yĩ sũ ni, yaana nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a. Baguma Aase Bati Kɔtɔ.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ nɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ sũ ni, yaana kɔtɔkase a kĩ Baguma aakpa ye a kamɛ. Ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ oti wĩ nɛ ayekĩ ɛnyɛ oti wĩ sũ ni, yaana tsɛfã nɛ kɔtɔkase a kĩ Baguma aakpa oti wĩ a odu.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ mi ni, ɛtɛ yĩ malo, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ yĩ ni, ɛtɛ oti a kĩ edo yĩ a.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Mitɛ mido kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaakpa bikĩ bitomɔ yĩ kĩĩ kamɛ obisɛ̃de mɔa mui dɔwa kɔɔpu mɔapɛ malo ni, yaana foe kɔtɔkase.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Kĩ Yohanes batɔ a dzakũ a, Yesu yi kayɔ mɔ kɔdzɛla kalɛ nɛ Yohanes akũ kabi bati kɔdabu a kĩ, “Kĩ minɛ minaa Yohanes sɛkɛ̃ nɛ kɔsa kamɛ a, litsa minaadã? Kɔɔkaɛ kĩ ɔwɛ abɔa katomɛna katɛ̃mɛna mbɔɛɛ?

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Kĩ Yohanes ya a, ɛtánya ɛtámɔa waĩ, lɛmɛ bati lɛ kĩ, ‘Hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ!’

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Kĩ Yesu do ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a fɔtɔ eyua a, ɛdzakũ nɛ awã ɛnaa tɛmatɔ a kĩ tifuã a kamɛ kĩ yanaatsa fɔtsa, ede lɛtsã nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a lɛmɛ akũ.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a, kĩ ɛwaa kɔla a nu lɛtsa a kĩ Yesu lamɛna kakɛna a, edo ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a badi nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃,

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: kĩ bɛnaavia ye kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ oti a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaaya a, mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɔdã oti bɛbã anɛ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mimuniki minaabi Yohanes lɛtsa a kĩ mimɔ́ mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ minu a.

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Banɛkunɛ mɔ́ fɔkɔ, bikĩ biveliveli yidza bɛtsɛ̃, bikĩ basɔ fɔsɔ ngbã akũ kpalɛ, batɔsĩnɛ anu fɔkɔ, bɛdzɛla banɔkpa bidzi nkpã, ade bɛlamɛna ahiãfɔ Kalɛ Wĩ a kabi a.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Baguma hila oti a kĩ ɛtámɔ nfumusu adima nɛ yĩ akũ!”

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Litsa koũ minɛ minaadã? Oti kĩ ɛkɛɛlɛ ye akũ mɔ fɔtsa mɔ kalɛ yanɛ? Bikĩ bado tɛtadiɛ mɔ kɔya ni, fɔgã tɔtɔ kamɛ bana.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Mibi yĩ lɛtsa a sũ koũ miti minɛ a? Kĩ míamɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ mbɔɛɛ? Nuwĩ mifɔ̃ nti mbi mi, mimɔ́ oti a kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ malo.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a bɛ̃ otidziwa odi nyɛ odi kĩ edzi nkpã tɔ̃. Lɛmɛ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ obisɛ̃ kɔlaa nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a bɛ̃ Yohanes.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: Kito Yohanes lɛtsã kade lɔkɔ kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ ni, bati yidza bɛdza nɛ Baguma ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, bɛlamɔ mbɔdi kabɔa lɛmɛ kĩ basua de kaya osi.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Ntɛ miawɛ kĩ miatɛ be fɔlɛtsa mido kɔ̃ ni, minyi kĩ Yohanes nyɛ Eliya a kĩ bɛlɛ biyi kĩ yaanya lɔtɔ ɛya a.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Finyaa, litsa maakɔa nviã bati nkɔnamɔ? Bɛla ndɛ babisɛ̃ kĩ bikoni nɛ kɔyaatɔ bagulo. Babɔa fɔwɔla kabi be bakɔba kĩ,

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Bɔsã obolitsɔ̃ tɔnɔ bɔkpa mi, lɛmɛ mitáto. Bɔsã tukutɔnɔ bɔkpa mi, lɛmɛ mitábe.’

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Kĩ Otidziwa Obi a ya a, yanya, yamɔa, ade bati lɛ kĩ, ‘Midã ɔnyakpanɛ mɔ bavɛ ɔmɔanɛ, tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ ɔkɔba.’ Lɛmɛ oti fɔkɛlatsa kamɛ bamɔ ye nyansã.” Tɛmatɔ a Kĩ Tɛtátɛ Bido a.

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Tɛmatɔ a kĩ te kamɛ Yesu kɛna ye awãwã fɔtsa pii a kamɛ bati tádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, foesũ ɛkã tɛmatɔ a anɛ ɛlɛ kĩ,

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Ade nɔ Kapernaum! Ɔwɛ kĩ abɔɛ nɔ akũ ɔdɛla ɔnaatamɔ ode? Baakɛna nɔ nɛ kayɔ flee ɔnaawo kanɔkpade! Kitonɛkĩ awãwã fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ nɔ kamɛ a mɔ Sodom bɛkɛna foe ni, kufɛ ɔmatɔ a buki edeĩ mɔ nviã kĩĩ!

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ ni, Baguma aamɔ Sodom ɔmatɔ a kɔnya ɛba nɔ.” Miya Nɛ Yĩ Sɛkɛ̃ Miyaawɛya.

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Yesu lɛ kĩ, “Nda, tite mɔ ode Ɔlate, manyɛ nɔ kɔlɛ kĩ ɔnyɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla tuvoli banyinɛ mɔ anyansãfɔ a ɔtsa babisɛ̃.

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Oo! Nda, ade foe awo nɔ anɛ a.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Nda kɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa edo nɛ yĩ awɛ. Odima linyína Obi a mbɔɛntɛ Ɔlɛga a odi, ade odima lɛmɛ linyína Ɔlɛga a mbɔɛntɛ Obi a odi, mɔ bikĩ Obi a awɛ kĩ yanyɛ ye ɛtsa a.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: “Miya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, mi flee a kĩ mikɛna adzuma, minɔnɛ fɔtsa mɔ ɔlɔmɛ kĩ kipiã mi a, lɔkɔa maakpa mi kawɛya.

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Mibɔɛ yĩ sɔmɔ akũ otse a mite nɛ mi akũ, mikɔlɛ fɔtsa mitomɛna yĩ sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa mina kawɛya, kitonɛkĩ yĩ fɔlɛtsa dɔ, ade ndɔla yĩ akũ nɛ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a.

ref: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
text: Kitonɛkĩ yĩ sɔmɔ akũ otse a kalɔ lɛlá ɔsĩ, ade yĩ fɔtsa a kĩ maalɔla mi a adã faafaa a.”

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Kĩ Farisifɔ mɔ́ foe a, bibi Yesu kĩ, “Dã, nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ akɛna lɛtsa a kĩ boe mbla tákpa osi kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ!”

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Lɛtsa a kĩ Yesu kɛna a, kɛna bati kɔdabu a flee a saa, ade bivia kĩ, “Mbɔɛɛ nnɛ́ Dawid Obi a nɛ awĩ a?”

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Oowo! Nnɛ́ Beelzebul kɔbɛ̃ nnɛɛ, mbom Baguma Hũhũ kɔbɛ̃ nnɛɛ mablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa, kĩ fɔatsa kĩ Baguma lɛgãkanya a ya nɛ mi akũ kɔkɔɛ.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Mbɔɛɛ mitákã nɛ Mose mbla ovoli a kamɛ kĩ nɛ Kawɛya luwi nyɛ luwi akũ ni, basɔfɔ a kĩ basum nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a afum Kawɛya Luwi mbla a, lɛmɛ baákɔ be foe kɔtɔ?

ref: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
text: Ewo nɛ Baguma kɔla a kamɛ ade bɛmɔ ye bati a bɛnya abodoo a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa Baguma a, titɔ kĩ mbla tákpa osi kĩ bɛnya ye malo. Basɔfɔ odi bɛkpa osi kĩ bɛnya abodoo a na.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, ‘Sɛmɛ kalɛ mawɛ, nnɛ́ bayaɛ afɔliɛ.’ Ntɛ kanu minu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kayɔ ni, kufɛ miláakɔ bikĩ bɛtáfum a kɔtɔ,

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Adeke ebi onyole a kĩ ye kɔkpɔ ku a kĩ, “Yo nɔ kɔkpɔ.” Eyo kɔkpɔ a ade kubuki kɔna ɔsĩ ndɛ kɔyade a.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Kĩ Yesu nu kĩ bisui nɛ ye akũ a, ɛdzakũ nɛ lɔpɔ a na kamɛ, lɛmɛ bati kɔdabu baũ tomɔ ye. Ɛtsa basɔnɛ a flee,

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: ade esi be mbla kĩ bɛtabi bati kadima nɛ ye akũ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Ɛlɛ fukĩĩ ayekĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ ɛtsãmɛna ye Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya akũ a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ,

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Ade yĩ osumunɛ a kĩ mpau nɛ awĩ a. Ɛnyɛ oti kĩ mawɛ kalɛ, ade yawo yĩ anɛ a. Maakɔa yĩ Hũhũ nko nɛ ye akũ, lɔkɔa ede lɛtsã nɛ yĩ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛ akũ ɛkpa fude fude.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ edzɛ́ nɛ yĩ liti ni, eyidza nɛ yĩ akũ, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaábumɔ yĩ bɔakpɔta ni, kayalɛ yayalɛ kawula.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Ke liti a, bati badi kpã oti odi kĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ, kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔnɛkunɛ mɔ omumu bɛma Yesu. Yesu tsa oti a, epuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla ɛmɔ fɔkɔ lɛmɛ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu nyi lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkakɔna a, foesũ ebi be kĩ, “Ɔmaĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛsɛ̃ ye akũ nɛ kutsu kutsu kamɛ, mɔ tɛdzɛ deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ ni, ɛláadzi gɛlɛɛlɛ. Ade aye a ntɛ ɔmatɔ ĩye kɔla kudi bati sɛ̃ nɛ kutsu kutsu kamɛ mɔ tɛdzɛ deĩ nɛ be kamɛ ni, kaku ɔmatɔ a ĩye kɔla a aaku.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Ntɛ mialɛ kĩ Beelzebul lɛkpa yĩ kɔbɛ̃ nnɛɛ mablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a ni, owei lɛkpa bati a kĩ bitomɔ mi a kɔbɛ̃ bɛnɛɛ bablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa? Lɛtsa a kĩ mi mɔawɛ bati a kĩ bitomɔ mi akɛna a, aakɔ mi kɔtɔ.

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
text: “Ntɛ hũhũ kpa nɛ́ nɛ oti kamɛ a, yanaatsã kamini nɛ fɔkɔ wɛlɛwa kawɛ kadzikɔ̃. Ntɛ ɛtána kadzikɔ̃ ni,

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
text: yanaa kanaakpã hũhũ kpa bɛbã evũkɔnɔ kĩ bideĩ ɔsĩ bɛbɛ̃ ye kayamɛna. Bawo nɛ oti a kamɛ kadzi nɛ awã, ade oti a kadzi kamɛ abu kade kaba ayekĩ fɔna nɛ kayɔkayi a. Ade aye fɔ́aya fɔkpa nviã bati kpa kĩĩ a.” Yesu Ɔma Mɔ Yesu Bawaɛ̃nyole.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Fɔmɔ aana kutse libi wĩ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ana kutse wĩ, ade ntɛ odeĩ kutse kpa ni, kutse libi kpa aana. Babi a kĩ kutse awũ a bakɔa kanyi kutse a odu.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mi bati ndɛ bɔɛ babi! Nde míapuli milɛ fɔlɛtsa wĩ kĩ minyɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ? Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ liyi oti ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ni, ade foe anɛ nɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: Mitɛ mido kĩ nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ ni, odi nyɛ odi aanyɛ ye akũ onukpɛ̃ nɛ kalɛ pupulidza kadi nyɛ kadi kĩ ɛlɛ tɔ̃ a akũ.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: Nɔ mɔawɛ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa baakɔa bɛdzɛmɔ nɔ bɛkpa nɔ ɔtɔbɛ ĩye bɛkɔ nɔ kɔtɔ.” Mbla Batsanɛ Mɔ Farisifɔ Via Awãwã Fɔtsa Bitomɛna Yesu Sɛkɛ̃.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Ade mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ badi bi Yesu kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bɔawɛ kĩ kɛna nsɛntsɛlɛni adi bɔdã.”

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nviã babi kpa mɔ bati kĩ binyína Baguma! Miavia yĩ kĩ nkɛna awãwã fɔtsa midã? Oowo! Awãwã fɔtsa a kĩ baanyɛ bɛtsa mi nyɛ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yona fude a.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Ndɛ ayekĩ Yona dzi fuwi ɛlalɛ lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa nɛ kafɔabi kpale lupu kamɛ a, ade aye Otidziwa Obi a lɛmɛ aatɛ fuwi ɛlalɛ lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa nɛ tite kamɛ a.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ ni, Ninivefɔ aayidza mɔ mi bidzi bɛkɔ mi kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kĩ binu Baguma fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Yona lɛ a. Midã, litsedi deĩ nɛ awĩ kĩ lɛbɛ̃ Yona.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ a, Seba Lɛgãtsole aayidza ɛdza ɛkɔ mi kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ ɛtsã osi gɛlɛɛlɛ eto ye ɔmaĩ kamɛ ɛyatse Lɛgã Salomo nyansã fɔtsa katsa, ade mitɛ yĩ mido kĩ odi deĩ nɛ awĩ kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Salomo. Hũhũ Kpa Kabuki Kamuniki Kaya.

ref: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.

text: Foesũ bati a kamɛ odi bi ye kĩ, “Dã, ama mɔ awaɛ̃nyole amɛ dzɛ̃ nɛ kanya bawɛ kĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ nɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”

ref: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
text: Ade eyo awɛ ɛtsa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ɛlɛ kĩ, “Midã, ade mma mɔ nwaɛ̃ amɛ nɛ awĩ a!

ref: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakɛna nɛ Nda nɛ ode kawɛ akũ ni, ade ye nyɛ nwaɛ̃nyole, mɔ nwaɛ̃tsole mɔ mma a.”

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Ayekĩ bati kɔdabu a kĩ kɔtsɛna nɛ ye akũ a tɛ́nɛ kapɔ sũ ni, ewo edzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ ade kɔdabu a dza nɛ ɔwɔ abuĩ a.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɛtsa be fɔtsa pii nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Luwi a onyole odi naasã kamɔnɛ.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Babi a badi tɔ nɛ apibɔ akũ awã kĩ tite lɛpɔ́nɛ nɛ boe a anɛ. Babi a mɛ mɔatsɛ kitonɛkĩ tite a kamɛ tádzũ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Babi a badi tɔ nɛ fu tuwĩ kamɛ ade kĩ bɛmɛ bade a tuwĩ a bo anɛ tɛmɛna be.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Ade fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Nde sũ abi bati a fɔlɛtsa nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ?”

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Kitonɛkĩ oti a kĩ edeĩ fɔtsa ni, baakpa ye fudi bibu lɔkɔa fɔpɔ fubu, lɛmɛ oti a kĩ ɛlá lɛtsama ni, baatɛ foe kabii a kĩ edeĩ a malo nɛ ye awɛ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Foesũ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a ya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, ‘Bati kĩĩ aanu bibuki binu lɛmɛ bɛláanu foe kayɔ. Ade baamɔ bibuki bɛmɔ́ lɛmɛ bɛláamɔ biyi ɔsũ.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ, Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ mɔ bati kpĩĩ pii wɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bamɔ lɛtsa a kĩ miamɔ a, lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bɛmɔ́ foe, ade bɛwɛ lɛmɛ kĩ banu lɛtsa a kĩ mianu a lɛmɛ bɛtánu foe. Yesu Nyɛ Lɛgbã Nɛ Kamɔnɛ a Odunɛ Akũ a Kayɔ.

ref: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once.
text: Babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ apibɔ akũ a nyɛ oti a kĩ ntɛ esila enu fɔlɛtsa a, yatɛ foe mɔatsɛ mɔ kawɔlɛ,

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: Babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ fu tuwĩ kamɛ a nyɛ oti a kĩ yanu fɔlɛtsa a, lɛmɛ katinya kamɛ ɔhau mɔ akũ kana fɔtsa liti katomɛna anya fɔlɛtsa a akũ, foesũ fɔáwũ babi.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
text: Ade babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ tite wĩ akũ a nyɛ oti a kĩ yanu fɔlɛtsa a, kanu foe kayɔ lɛmɛ. Bawũ babi, badi ɔha, badi aduesia babɛbã lɛmɛ be taalɛ.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Tuwĩ Wuluwulu Akũ.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã bɛbã kĩ, “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ oti odi kĩ ɛsã kamɔnɛ babi wĩ nɛ ye kɔba kamɛ.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Nɛ lɛnyɛ mɔa lidi, kĩ odi nyɛ odi wa tida a, ye okesĩnɛ nasã tuwĩ wuluwulu nɛ kamɔnɛ a kamɛ ɛdzakũ.

ref: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
text: Kĩ kamɔnɛ a bo anɛ kido kayɛlɛ awɛ a, tuwĩ a lɛmɛ nɛ́ tɛdza nɛ ke kamɛ.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: “Oti a basumunɛ ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bibi ye kĩ, ‘Boe ɔlate, nnɛ́ funitsã wĩ odu nɛ kɔba a kamɛ, mɔ ndia tuwĩ kpa to tɛyamɛ nɛ ke kamɛ?’

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
text: “Ɔlate a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Okesĩnɛ odi lɛkɛna fukĩna.’ “Adeke bivia ye kĩ, ‘Awɛ kĩ bɔnaabɔlɛ tuwĩ a nɛ ke kamɛ?’

ref: “No!” he answered. “You might also pull up the wheat.
text: “Ebi be kĩ, ‘Oowo! Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ minaabɔlɛ tuwĩ a ni, míabɔlɛ kamɔnɛ a kadi mɔ te.

ref: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
text: Lɛnyɛ kutse libi kabii flee nɛ kutse babi kamɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ libo anɛ ni, kɔapɔ lububu kaba futse flee, ade kɔvɔlabi ayakɛna fɔtsɔɛ nɛ koe tɛnanɛ kamɛ a.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Oyila Fɔtsa Akũ.

ref: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
text: Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã bɛbã kĩ, “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ kutse libi kabii kĩ oti odi nadu nɛ ye kɔba kamɛ.

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Yesu bi bati kɔdabu a fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ, ade yaálɛ fɔlɛtsa fudima ntɛ nnɛ́ nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
text: Ɛkɛna fukĩĩ kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa a fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, “Maalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ, ade maanyɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla kito anɛ katinya kayɔkayi a ntsa a.” Yesu Nyɛ Lɛgbã Nɛ Tuwĩ Wuluwulu Akũ a Kayɔ.

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Kĩ Yesu nyɛ bati kɔdabu a osi a, ɛya nɛ kɔla kamɛ ade ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bɛyavia ye kĩ, “Nyɛ lɛgbã a kĩ ɔbɔa nɛ tuwĩ wuluwulu nɛ kamɔnɛ kamɛ a kayɔ katsa boe.”

ref: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
text: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oti a kĩ edu fudutsa wĩ a nyɛ Otidziwa Obi a.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Kɔba a nyɛ katinya kamɛ, fudutsa wĩ a nyɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a bade, ade tuwĩ a lɛmɛ nyɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ Okesĩnɛ a bade a.

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: Okesĩnɛ a kĩ ɛsã tuwĩ a nyɛ Abonsam. Fudutsa kakpɔta a nyɛ katinya kamɛ kawũna lɔkɔ, ade fudutsa bakpɔtanɛ a nyɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: “Ndɛ ayekĩ bakpɔta tuwĩ kapila te a, ade aye fɔ́ana nɛ katinya kawũna lɔkɔ a.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.

text: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mikɔa foe mima yĩ nɛ awĩ.”

text: Ade fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ ado oti efĩĩ a, lɛmɛ kĩ ɔkɔa awɛ kĩ ɔtávɔlɛ anya ndɛ ayekĩ Farisifɔ atsa a, ládo oti efĩĩ.” Otsole Odi Katɛkado.

text: Kĩ Yesu nu foe a, ɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “O, boe ɔma, nɔ katɛkado pɔnɛ! Lɛtsa a kĩ awɛ a lɛya lɛkpa nɔ.” Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ a obitsole a akũ de ɔsĩ. Yesu Tsa Bati Beblebee.
