text: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã ɛtsã Galilea ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a abuĩ. Ede ɛnaa katɔbii kadi akũ ade ɛkɛna akũ edzi a.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Kĩ bati kɔdabu a mɔ́ kĩ bamumu kalɛ kɔdzɛla, abafã amɛ kayidza katsã, bikĩ bɛnɛ lɛkpɛ̃ akũ dani fuyo, banɛkunɛ kamɔ fɔkɔ a, fɔkɛna be wãwã, ade bimu Israel Baguma a. Yesu Kpa Bati Mpim Ɛna Funitsã Bɛnya.

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yesu vɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ade ebi be kĩ, “Bati kĩĩ akũ akɔ yĩ kɔnya, kitonɛkĩ bɛna nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ fuwi ɛlalɛ flee, lɛmɛ bɛlá lɛtsama kĩ banya. Ntáwɛ kĩ manyɛ be osi bɛnaa mɔ kɔlɛ, anɛ lɛnaasinya be nɛ osi.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via ye kĩ, “Nɛ ndia bɔ́ana funitsã nɛ kɔsa kamɛ nɛ awĩ kĩ fɔ́ato bati kɔdabu kĩĩ odu bɛnya?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yesu via be kĩ, “Abodoo eni minɛɛ?” Bibi ye kĩ, “Abodoo evũkɔnɔ mɔ kɔfɔabi wuluwulu sukuloo deĩ.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Ade Yesu fɔ̃ bati kɔdabu a kɛna akũ bidzi nɛ tite a.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Ade kĩ ɛbɔɛ abodoo evũkɔnɔ mɔ kɔfɔabi a ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ a, ebulibuli ye mɔ koe ɛkɔa ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a, ade be malo bɛsɛ̃ bɛkpa bati a.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Be flee bɛnya biwĩ. Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a fila fuwuliwa a kĩ fute a, fuyi tɛtsɛtsɛ evũkɔnɔ.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Ke liti a, Yesu nyɛ bati a osi ade edzi ɔkɔlɔ ɛnaa Magadan amantam kamɛ a.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Nde sũ mɔ miámɔ kĩ nnɛ́ abodoo fɔlɛtsa nlamɛna mi kabi? Midã kadã wĩ nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ fuyilatsa akũ!”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Kĩ Yesu wo kakɔ a kĩ kifuã Kaisarea Filipi ɔmatɔ a, evia ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Owei bati alɛ kĩ Otidziwa Obi a anɛ?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Ade finyaa fɔmɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yanu foe kayɔ kĩ nnɛ́ fuyilatsa a kĩ bakɔa kado nɛ mawɛ kamɛ a akũ yadɛ be, mbom kadɛ ɛdɛ be nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ fɔtsa katsa a akũ. Petro Nyɛ Yesu Ɛtsa Ndɛ Oti a Kĩ Bɛpau a.

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Lɛmɛ Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ suwa anaa katomukɔ̃ a, mialɛ kĩ, ‘Fɔkɔ aakpalɛ kunya, kitonɛkĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ yɔɔlɛ.’

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Ade ayea ɔnɔnɛ lɛmɛ mialɛ kĩ, ‘Nviã oni aako kitonɛkĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ yɔɔlɛ fɔkɔ lɛmɛ kɛna tũũ.’ Miayi anɛ kadã odetɔ́ kamɛ kapuli kalɛ ayekĩ fɔkɔ aana, lɛmɛ miápuli minyi nviã nsɛntsɛlɛni a kĩ yaaya a kayɔ.

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Badi alɛ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a anɛ. Babɛbã alɛ kĩ Eliya, ade badi lɛmɛ alɛ kĩ Yeremia ĩye Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa anɛ.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Kito luwi a, Yesu do ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kabi awɛ pefee kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ nnaa Yerusalem, nnaamɔ lubo paa nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a awɛ. Baakɔ yĩ, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ ni, Baguma aadzɛla yĩ ndzi nkpã.”

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mite anɛ, midã kadã wĩ nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ fuyilatsa a akũ.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Ade bido kalɛ nɛ be nɛɛnɛɛ kamɛ kĩ, “Ayekĩ bɔtábɔɛ abodoo adima bɔlɛ sũ mɔ ɛlɛ foe.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a aaya nɛ Ɔlɛga kukũkpalɛ kamɛ mɔ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ, lɔkɔa yaase odi nyɛ odi kɔtɔ nɛ ayekĩ edzi nkpã a onukpɛ̃.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Nɛ foesũ, Petro, mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ nɔ dĩ kayɔ nyɛ de mɔ ɔsĩ a, ade de kĩĩ akũ maayi yĩ asɔli a. Wuda malo lɛláapuli ɛnya ye akũ da.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Yesu via be kĩ, “Adeke mi malo, owei mialɛ kĩ ye manɛ?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Ade Simon Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Kristo a, Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a Obi.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Simon, Yona obi, Baguma hila nɔ, kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ otidziwa odima lɛtsa nɔ foe, mbom Nda nɛ ode a lɛnyɛ foe ɛtsa nɔ.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Maakɔa ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a tɛsafi ndo nɔ nɛ awɛ. Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ osĩ nɛ tite akũ ni, baasĩ foe nɛ ode, ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ ɔkpa osi nɛ tite akũ ni, baakpa de osi nɛ ode.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Adeke Yesu si ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mbla kĩ bɛtabi odima kĩ ye nyɛ Kristo a. Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Ye Lubokamɔ Mɔ Wuda Akũ.

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Petro kpã Yesu bɛnaadza nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃, ade edo ye anɛ kakã awɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, Baguma esua foe osi. Fukĩnaa lɛláaya nɛ nɔ akũ da!”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Yesu dani ɛtsɛlamɔ Petro ebi ye kĩ, “Satana, dzakũ nɛ yĩ akũ! Ɔnyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lasua yĩ osi kitonɛkĩ nɔ kakɔna táto Baguma sɛkɛ̃ mbom futo otidziwa sɛkɛ̃.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Ade Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Ntɛ odi wɛ kĩ yatomɔ yĩ ni, ɛtatsɛla ye mɔawɛ akũ lɔkɔa ɛbɔɛ ye asendua etomɔ yĩ.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ kĩ yapili ye nkpã ni, yaayɛla ye, lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛyɛla ye nkpã nɛ yĩ sũ ni, yaana ye.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Ntɛ katinya kamɛ fɔtsa flee dani odi fude mɔ ɛyɛla ye nkpã ni, litsa nfasɔ yaana? Mbɔɛɛ litsedi deĩ kĩ oti aakɔa ɛvɛmɛna mɔ ye nkpã?

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Mabi mi pipiipi kĩ badi deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ nɛ awĩ kĩ bɛláaku kɛnaawo kĩ bɛmɔ́ Otidziwa Obi a kĩ ɛya ndɛ Lɛgã aye.”

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Kĩ bɛkadã ye a, Yesu akũ dani nɛ be anɛ kamɛ. Ye anɛ kamɛ kata ndɛ suwa aye, ade ye tɛtadiɛ lɛmɛ kɛna fitaa ndɛ ɔhaĩ aye a.

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: Ade awã mɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu foe kayɔ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a akũ kɔdzɛla ɛkalɛ. Yesu Tsa Kanyolebi Kadi Kĩ Hũhũ Kpa Na Nɛ Ke Kamɛ.

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Nɛ fuwi evũ liti a, Yesu kpã Petro, Yakobo mɔ ɔwaɛ̃ Yohanes bide bɛnaa ɔtɔ geĩ odi kalete, awã kĩ be odi bɛnadzi.

ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: Adeke fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ɛlalɛ a mɔ́ Mose mɔ Eliya kĩ bɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ Yesu a.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Foesũ Petro dzɛla odo ebi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, fɔnɛnɛ paa kĩ budeĩ nɛ awĩ! Ntɛ awɛ ni, maabɔa fɔpɛ ɛlalɛ nɛ awĩ, nɔ lide lɔmɔa, Mose lide lɔmɔa lɔkɔa Eliya lɛmɛ lide lɔmɔa.”

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Kĩ ɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a, luflututu fitaa ka lɛtsa be, ade odo nɛ́ eto de kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade yĩ Obi kĩ maátunɔ ye kalɛ a, yawo yĩ anɛ. Mitse ye!”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu odo a libe wo be paa, foesũ bɛka bɛtsa anɛ nɛ tite.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Yesu ya nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ ɛkɔa awɛ ɛta be, ade ebi be kĩ, “Miyidza! Mitavila!”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Foesũ bɛdzɛla anɛ nɛ ode, lɛmɛ bɛtámɔ odima nɛ awã kɛba Yesu.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Adeke fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via Yesu kĩ, “Nde sũ mbla batsanɛ a alɛ kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ Eliya ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya?”

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ Eliya ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya, ɛyaalɔla lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa eyi.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Mabi mi kĩ Eliya ya kɔkɔɛ lɛmɛ bati támɔ ye biyi ɔsũ, foesũ bɛkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ bawɛ nɛ ye akũ. Ade ayea baakɛna Otidziwa Obi a lɛmɛ awɔda a.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a muniki bɛya nɛ bati kɔdabu a sɛkɛ̃ a, onyole odi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ɛyake aduli nɛ ye anɛ,

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, mɔ yĩ obinyole kɔnya! Odetɔ́ fɔsɔ deĩ nɛ ye akũ fɔamɔna ye lubo mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ. Fɔfɔ̃ yawɛ katɔ nɛ fi ĩye mui kamɛ.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Nkpã ye mma nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bɛtsa ye.”

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “O mi nviã babi kpa kĩ milá katɛkado. Mbɔɛɛ maana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ daa? Lɔkɔ lɔɔdabe kamɛ maana ɔwɔlɛ nvɛlɛ mi nnaawo? Mikpã kanyolebi a mima yĩ nɛ awĩ!”

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Adeke Yesu wũni nɛ hũhũ kpa a akũ mɔ ɛnɛ nɛ kanyolebi a kamɛ, ade kɛna akũ ɔsĩ nɛ lɔkɔ mɔapɛ a na kamɛ a.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: Ke liti a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ nɛ kabɛla kamɛ bivia ye kĩ, “Nde sũ boe kɔ̃ bɔtápuli bɔblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a bɔnɛmɛna?”

ref: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
text: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a flee yakpɔta nɛ Galilea a, Yesu bi be kĩ, “Baakɔa Otidziwa Obi a bido nɛ bati awɛ.

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: Baakɔ ye, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ a, Baguma aadzɛla ye edzi nkpã.” Nɛ foesũ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo ɔblɔ paa. Yesu Mɔ Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Se Asɔli Kɔla Ɔgɔlɛ.

ref: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
text: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo Kapernaum a, bikĩ batɛ Asɔli Kɔla ɔgɔlɛ a ya nɛ Petro sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Mi fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ a ase Asɔli Kɔla ɔgɔlɛ?”

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, yase.” Kĩ Petro wo ɛnaa tɔtɔ kamɛ a, Yesu nya lɔtɔ evia ye kĩ, “Simon, litsa nyɛ nɔ adzuni? Baáde ase dutɛ ĩye tɔgɔlɛ kakpa katinya kamɛ fɔgã? Kade a bati ase mbɔɛɛ kĩ banɔvɔɛ̃?”

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Banɔvɔɛ̃ a.” Ade Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ fɔatsa kĩ kade bati a wɛya.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Lɛmɛ bɔáwɛ kĩ bɔafum bati kĩĩ, foesũ naata dalɛwa nɛ ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a kamɛ. Kafɔabi katɔnyade a kĩ aanyɛ a ni, daɛ ke onukpɛ̃, aamɔ sika ovoe kĩ saato bɔmɔ nɔ Asɔli Kɔla ɔgɔlɛ kase. Kɔa se kanaase be bɔmɔ nɔ tɔgɔlɛ.”

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Ade ayea ntɛ nɔ linebi aafɔ̃ ɔkɛna tɔkpa ni, bɔlɛ de kanyɛ kata kamuni! Fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ kĩ aana linebi mɔa odzi nkpã, kɛba kĩ aana banebi nviã lɛmɛ bɛta nɔ bido nɛ fi a kĩ fɔádĩ da a kamɛ a. Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Kuveli Kĩ Kɔyɛla Akũ.

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Kɛtáfɛta kĩ amɔ nɔ ɔkɔba ɔsumunɛ kɔnya ndɛ ayekĩ mmɔ́ nɔ kɔnya a?’

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Oti kĩ ɛbɛ̃ flee nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a nyɛ oti a kĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ, ɛdani ɛkɛna ndɛ obisɛ̃ kĩĩ aye.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaakɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ ɛkpa obisɛ̃ kĩĩ odu nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, yĩ ɛkɛna ɛkpa. Takpã Odima Odo Nɛ Tɔkpa Kamɛ.

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: Lɛmɛ ntɛ esĩ ɛtátse be malo ni, kɔa fɔlɛtsa a kama asɔli a, ade ntɛ ɛtátse asɔli bati a lɛmɛ ni, bu ye ndɛ ɔmamuni ĩye ɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ aye. Yesu Kpa Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Otumi.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: “Ntɛ oti odi aakpã babisɛ̃ kĩĩ, kĩ bɛtɛ yĩ bido a kamɛ ɔmɔa edo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ ni, fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa ye fɔbɛ̃ kĩ bɛkɔa kɔwɔlɛ kpale bɛvɛla nɛ ye sɔmɔ bɛta bido nɛ mpo kamɛ ɛmɔa mui eku.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: “Foesũ ntɛ nɔ awɛ ĩye nɔ kɔbɔa aafɔ̃ ɔkɛna tɔkpa ni, tsua koe kata kamuni! Fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa nɔ kĩ aana nkpã mɔ awɛ mɔa ĩye kɔbɔa mɔa, kɛba kĩ aana akpɔ mɔ abɔa lɛmɛ bɛta nɔ bido nɛ fi a kĩ fɔádĩ da a kamɛ.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: “Ntɛ awaɛ̃ fum nɔ ni, naa ye sɛkɛ̃ kanaanyɛ ye mfumsu katsa ye, lɛmɛ kɛna foe nɛ kabɛla kamɛ nɛ mimɔ ye odi kayite. Ntɛ enu foe kayɔ ɛkpa nɔ ni, ɔdani ɔtɛ awaɛ̃.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a ya kĩ yaatɛ bikĩ bɛyɛla a bɛna nkpã.

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: Ntɛ ɛmɔ́ koe ni, yamɔ kawɔlɛ paa nɛ kɔmɔa a akũ kaba be aduekrɔũ evedi a kĩ bɛtáyɛla a.

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Ade ayea mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a láwɛ kĩ babisɛ̃ kĩĩ kamɛ odima ɛyɛla a. Kɛna Ɔmɔa Mɔ Awaɛ̃ Kĩ Efum Nɔ.

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛtánu ye akũ kayɔ ni, kpã oti mɔa ĩye bati nviã kabumɔ nɔ akũ minaa ye sɛkɛ̃, ayekĩ fɔlɛtsa a flee aanɛ fɔwa nɛ adansifɔ anɛ.

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: “Foesũ mabi mi flee kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ mitákpa osi nɛ tite akũ ni, bɛláakpa de osi nɛ ode, ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ míakpa osi nɛ tite akũ ni, baakpa de osi nɛ ode.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Mabi mi mbu kĩ ntɛ mi kamɛ bati nviã mikɛna ɔmɔa nɛ litsedi akũ nɛ tite akũ, mɔ mibɔa mpaɛ mivia de ni, Nda nɛ ode a aakɛna foe ɛkpa mi.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: Kitonɛkĩ awã kĩ bati nviã ĩye be ɛlalɛ tsɛna nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, ndeĩ nɛ be kayite.” Lɛgbã Nɛ Ɔsumunɛ Kĩ Ɛtámɔ Ɔkɔba Kɔnya a Akũ.

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Ke liti a, Petro yavia Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, ntɛ nwaɛ̃ dzɛmɛna tɔkpa kakɛna nɛ yĩ akũ ni, titi eni kɛnɛ kĩ nkɔa nte ye? Titi evũkɔnɔ mbɔɛɛ?”

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Kĩ esila eyi kayɔ a, bɛkpã be kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ edzimɔ ye sika mpim mpim kɔtɔ bɛyamɛna.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Ɔsumunɛ a lɛlá sika kĩ yaase kɔtɔ a, foesũ lɛgã a kpa kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bɛnɔ ye mɔawɛ a ndɛ ɔsate, ye ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ babi, mɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ edeĩ a bɛkɔa bise kɔtɔ a.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: “Ɔsumunɛ a ke aduli nɛ lɛgã a anɛ eke ye aduli kĩ, ‘Na ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kakpa yĩ. Maase nɔ se flee.’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Ye akũ kɔ lɛgã a kɔnya foesũ ɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ ade ɛkɔa kɔtɔ a kĩ edzimɛna a flee ete ye a.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: “Ɔkɔba a tɔ nɛ ye abɔa kamɛ eke ye aduli ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Na ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kakpa yĩ! Maase nɔ kɔtɔ a!’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: “Lɛmɛ ɛtátunɔ, mbom ɛfɔ̃ bido ye nɛ kɔla kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ yaase kɔtɔ a.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Kĩ basumunɛ bɛbã a mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a, fuwo be kalɔɛ ade bɛnabi lɛgã a lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a flee a.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Foesũ lɛgã a vɛ ɔsumunɛ a ɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ade ebi ye kĩ, ‘Nɔ ɔsumunɛ kpa! Nkɔa kɔtɔ beblebee a kĩ odzimɔ yĩ a nte nɔ nɛ kĩ oke yĩ aduli sũ.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Ade lɛgã a na kalɔkpa mɔ ɔsĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛnado ye nɛ kɔla kĩ bɛmɔna ye lubo kɛnaawo kĩ ese kɔtɔ a flee.”

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Yesu wũna kĩ, “Ade aye Nda nɛ ode a aakɛna mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtákɔa ete ɔwaɛ̃ etomɛna ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Kĩ Yesu lɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ eyua a, ɛdzakũ nɛ Galilea ɛnaa Yudea amantam nɛ Yordan ɔwɔ liti a.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Kitonɛkĩ kayɔkanyɛ futefute deĩ kĩ foe sũ banyole lɛláapuli bɛkɔã batsɔ̃. Badi bɛma be basaɛ, babɛbã bati lɛkɛna be basaɛ, ade badi lɛmɛ lákɔã batsɔ̃ nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a sũ. Oti a kĩ yaapuli ɛtɛ fɔtsa katsa kĩĩ ni, ɛtɛ foe.” Yesu Hila Kubisɛ̃ɛ̃.

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Bati kɔdabu pii tomɔ ye bɛnaa ade ɛtsa be fɔtsa nɛ awã a.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Farisifɔ a badi ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, ade bɛwɛ kĩ basɔ ye bɛdã, foesũ bivia ye kĩ, “Boe mbla kpa onyole osi kĩ esĩ ɔtsɔ̃ nɛ kalɛ nyɛ kalɛ kĩ yawɛ a akũ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mitákã nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ nɛ kayɔkayi a, otsole mɔ onyole Ɔbɔanɛ bɔa?

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: Ade Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ onyole aafɔnɛ ɔlɛga mɔ ɔma eyi ɛkɔa akũ ɛmɛna mɔ ɔtsɔ̃, lɔkɔa be nviã a baakɛna oti mɔa.’

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Foesũ bibuki bɛnyɛ́ bati nviã mbom bɛkɛna oti mɔa. Nɛ foesũ otidziwa ɛtasɛ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma boomu a kamɛ.”

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Ade Farisifɔ a via ye kĩ, “Mɔ nde sũ Mose kpa osi kĩ onyole ɛkpa ye ɔtsɔ̃ kasĩ ovoli lɔkɔa ɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ?”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ade mi atɔ ɔsĩ sũ mɔ Mose kpa mi osi kĩ misĩ mi batsɔ̃ a, lɛmɛ fɔtána lɔmɔ nɛ kayɔkayi a.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Lɛmɛ finyaa mabi mi kĩ ntɛ onyole odi sĩ ɔtsɔ̃ kĩ nnɛ́ kanɛ ɛnɛ ye liti sũ, mɔ ɛnakɔã otsole bɛbã ni, ɛbɔa adɔpaa.”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nnɛ́ odi nyɛ odi aapuli enu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kayɔ, mbɔɛntɛ bikĩ Baguma kpa be foe a.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Bati badi kpã babisɛ̃ bɛma Yesu kĩ ete awɛ nɛ be akũ, ɛbɔa mpaɛ ɛkpa be, lɛmɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wũni nɛ be akũ.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Luwi a onyole odi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ɛvia ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, lɛtsa wĩ lɔɔdabe kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna mɔ maana daa nkpã a?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Oti a via ye kĩ, “Tɛbla tɛwɔdabe?” Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Takɔ oti, tabɔa adɔpaa, taye, tadi adansiɛ klumu,

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: Adeke Yesu te awɛ nɛ be akũ ehila be, ade ɛdzakũ nɛ awã a. Opopo Osikani a.

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nde sũ avia yĩ kalɛ nɛ fɔtsa wĩ kakɛna akũ? Oti mɔapɛ nyɛ owĩ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ awɛ kĩ awo nɛ nkpã kamɛ kɔ̃ ni, kɛna nɛ tɛbla a akũ.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Lɛmɛ Yesu lɛ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ babisɛ̃ a bɛya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, mitasua be osi, kitonɛkĩ ade bikĩĩ bamɛ odu lá ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Lɛmɛ bati pii kĩ bɛnyɛ batɔnyade finyaa a, aakɛna liti bade, ade liti bade a aakɛna batɔnyade a.”

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: bu aga mɔ ama, kawɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ nɔ akũ kalɛ a.”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Ade opopo a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nkɛna nɛ tɛbla kĩĩ flee akũ. Litsa libuki lisĩ kĩ nkɛna?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ awɛ kĩ akpɔla ni, naa kanaanɔ nɔ akũ fɔtsa flee, kakɔa sika a kate ahiãfɔ, lɔkɔa aana akũ kana fɔtsa nɛ ode. Ke liti muniki kayaatomɔ yĩ.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Kĩ opopo a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, ɛdzakũ mɔ ayɔlɔhɔ, kitonɛkĩ edeĩ akũ fɔtsa beblebee.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Adeke Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ fɔ́ana ɔsĩ paa fɔkpa osikani kĩ yaawo nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Mbuki mabi mi kĩ fɔadã bɔɛɛ fɔkpa ɔpɔnkɔ mɔ kanɛ mɔ lɔpɔ kĩ yaatsã ɔnɔbɔ̃ ɔkɛ kamɛ ɛnɛ́ kɛba kĩ osikani aawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu kalɛ kĩĩ a, akũ dzuli be, ade bivia kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ owei aapuli ɛna katɛ?”

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Yesu dã be kɛnanaana ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Otidziwa lɛláapuli ɛkɛna fukĩĩ, lɛmɛ Baguma kɔ̃ apuli kakɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa.”

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Ade Petro dzɛla odo ɛlɛ kĩ, “Dã kĩ bɔfɔnɛ boe fɔtsa flee awɛ bɔya butomɔ nɔ, litsa koũ aanya boe fude?”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ ntɛ Otidziwa Obi a kɛna akũ edzi nɛ ye lɛgãkpo kpalɛwa a akũ nɛ katinya vɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ni, mi bati ewua-nviã a kĩ mitomɔ yĩ a lɛmɛ míadzi nɛ fɔgãkpo ewua-nviã akũ midzɛmɔ Israel futete ewua-nviã a.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: Ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛfɔnɛ fɔla, bawaɛ̃nyole, ĩye bawaɛ̃tsole, balɛga ĩye bama ĩye babi, ĩye fɔba biyi nɛ yĩ sũ ni, Baguma aakpa ye foe titi ɔha mɔ nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ okuani odi kĩ eyidza mɔ ɔnɔnɛ tutuutu ɛnabɔɛ apaafɔ badi kĩ bɛyaakɛna adzuma bɛkpa ye nɛ ye waĩ kɔba kamɛ.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Bɛmɔ apaafɔ a bɛnya kɔya, etunɔ kĩ yaase be sika ovoe sɔmɔa sɔmɔa kĩ fɔnyɛ luwi mɔa kɔtɔkase, ade edo be nɛ ye waĩ kɔba a kamɛ a.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: “Kĩ ɔnɔnɛ tɔdɔũ evedi bɔa a, onyole a buki ɛnɛ ɛnaa kɔya ade ɛnamɔ banyole badi kĩ bɛdzɛ̃, kĩ bɛlá adzuma kɛnawa adima.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Foesũ ebi be kĩ, ‘Mi malo minaakɛna adzuma nɛ yĩ waĩ kɔba kamɛ, maase mi kɔtɔ kĩ kɛfɛta a.’

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: Ade bɛnaa kɔba a. “Ebuki ɛnɛ nɛ tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã mɔ olobe tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ ɛnakɛna ndɛ fɔtɔnyade a.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Kĩ tɔdɔũ elo anaa kabɔakɔ̃ a, ɛnaa kɔya ɛnamɔ banyole bɛbã kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ pupulidza. Evia be kĩ, ‘Nde sũ midzɛ̃ nɛ awĩ luwi a flee kĩ miákɛna lɛtsama?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: “Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Odima tábɔɛ boe kĩ bɔkɛna adzuma bɔkpa ye.’ “Foesũ ebi be kĩ, ‘Ntɛ aye ni, mi malo minaakɛna adzuma nɛ yĩ kɔba a kamɛ.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Kĩ olobe wo ɔtɔ a, kɔba ɔlate a bi ye adzuma akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a kĩ, ‘Vɛ adzuma bakɛnanɛ a kase be kɔtɔ, kayi kayɔ katomɛna bikĩ bɛya nkaɛ a akũ kanaawũna nɛ batɔnyade a akũ.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: “Bikĩ biyi adzuma kayɔ nɛ olobe tɔdɔũ elo kamɛ a kamɛ odi nyɛ odi tɛ sika ovoe sɔmɔa sɔmɔa.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Kĩ bɛtɛ be sika a biyua a, bɛna kalɔkpa bɛkakɛna hũhũhũhũ nɛ ɔlate a akũ.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ade bɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Bati a kĩ ɔbɔɛ nkaɛ a kɛna adzuma dɔũhuilɛ mɔapɛ, ade boe kɔ̃ bɔdza nɛ suwa kpaa kamɛ luwi a flee bɔkɛna adzuma a, lɛmɛ ose boe flee kɔtɔ tsɛtsɛɛ!’

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: “Kɔba ɔlate a kpa be kamɛ ɔmɔa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Ɔkɔba, dã ntásisi nɔ. Nnɛ́ sika ovoe sɔmɔa akũ bɔnya kɔya?

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Kɔa nɔ kɔtɔkase kanaa kɔla. Yĩ lɛwɛ kĩ mase bati nkaɛ kĩĩ kɔtɔ ndɛ ayekĩ nse nɔ a.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Ndeĩ osi kĩ makɛna yĩ sika akũ adzuma ndɛ ayekĩ mawɛ. Mbɔɛɛ ayekĩ nkɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ a sũ anɛ abɛɛlɛ nɔ?’ ”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Ade Yesu wũna kĩ, “Foesũ bati a kĩ bɛkpa liti a, aadani batɔnyade, ade bikĩ bɛnyaa lɔtɔ a aadani batide a.” Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Lɛlalɛde Nɛ Ye Wuda Akũ.

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yesu fɔ̃ ɛdza ɛvɛ be kĩ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃. Evia be kĩ, “Litsa miawɛ kĩ nkɛna nkpa mi?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Be akũ kɔ Yesu kɔnya, ade ɛkɔa awɛ ɛta be banebi a. Deedimɔapɛ be anɛ binya bɛmɔ́ fɔkɔ ade bitomɔ ye a.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Bati kɔdabu a wũni nɛ be akũ kĩ bɛfɔ̃ fɔwɔla kabɔa, lɛmɛ bɛbɔa fɔwɔla a kɔɔba bɛba bɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, Dawid Obi, mɔ́ boe kɔnya ni!”

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:

text: Kĩ bati kɔdabu a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, akũ dzuli be nɛ ye fɔtsa katsa a akũ. Yesu Tsa Mbla a Kĩ Ɛbɛ̃ Flefle a.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.

text: “Makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ bɔbɔɛ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Mitsã mpo akũ minyina minaa fude fude kĩ miawɛ oti mɔapɛ ɛdani Yudani, lɛmɛ ntɛ midani ye a, miakɛna ye titi nviã ɛkpɔla ɛkpa fi luwotu kamɛ kanaa ndɛ mi aye!

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ mianɛ! text: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Mike funi mɔ kalɛ mikpa Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a ade mihɛhɛ bikĩ bidzi nkpã wĩ a anɛ kate fɔtsa a.

text: “Mi bati kĩ bɛla ndɛ bɔɛ babi! Kakɔna mikɔna kĩ míapuli milo, kĩ bɛláakɔ mi kɔtɔ bɛkpa fi luwotu kamɛ kanaa?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ nɛ wuda kĩĩ flee akũ aaya nɛ mi nviã babi akũ! Yesu Ɔdɔ Kĩ Ɛna Ɛkpa Yerusalem.

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Kĩ Yesu nɛ́ nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ yanaa a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ kĩ batsa ye ayekĩ bike kɔla a flee a.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Midã fukĩĩ flee kadã wĩ. Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ bɛláatina de lidima nɛ lɔkɔba akũ kĩ bɛláadu bimuni.” Yesu Bi Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Kĩ Baana Ɔhau Mɔ Liti Katomɛna.

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Míanu kawũ kanɔ mɔ kawũ akũ fɔlɛtsa mɔ lugo. Lɛmɛ mitafɔ̃ fɔtsa kĩĩ lɛbɔlɛ mi tɔwɔlɛ. Kɛnɛ kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ, lɛmɛ fɔátsa kĩ katinya kamɛ kawũna a wo ɔtɔ.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Kĩ Yesu nɛ edzi nɛ Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ a akũ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛla bɔɛɛ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Lɔkɔ lɔwɔdabe fɔtsa kĩĩ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ. Bi boe lɛtsa a kĩ laanya nɔ kaya mɔ katinya kamɛ kawũna a nsɛntsɛlɛni.”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Ɔmaĩ aanɔ mɔ akũ, ade fɔgã kanyakɔ̃ aayidza bɛnɔ mɔ fɔgã kanyakɔ̃ a. Kɔlɛ aana, ade tite aakuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ nɛ fɔkɔ pii a.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.

text: Ade deedimɔapɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa wula atsɛ ɛnabɔɛ ɔsapɔ edo nɛ waĩ yilawa kamɛ ɛkɔa ɛvɛla nɛ otse onukpɛ̃, ɛdɛla ete ye nɛ onukpɛ̃ kĩ ɛmɔa.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Adeke lima kpale a kĩ bɛvɛla nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a bɛ̃ fɔkɛ nviã, kito nyangɛ kɛyawo kayɔ. Tite kuna boe bakpale bakpale yɛlɛ,

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: funikɛ binya ade bɛdzɛla bati kpalɛwa pii kĩ biku a bidzi nkpã a.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Kĩ Yesu yidza eto kanɔkpa liti a, bati kpalɛwa a nɛ́ nɛ funikɛ a kamɛ bɛnaa Yerusalem, ɔmatɔ kpalɛwa a kamɛ, awã kĩ bati pii mɔ be.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Kĩ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a mɔ bikĩ bibumɔ ye bɛkadzɔlɛ Yesu akũ a mɔ́ tite kakuna mɔ fɔtsa bɛbã kĩ fɔya a, libe wo be paa ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali obi kĩĩ nyɛ Baguma Obi!”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Batsole pii kĩ bito Galilea, kĩ bitómɔ Yesu bɛkabumɔ ye a, dza mɔ lugo bɛkadã.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Ɛnaa Pilato sɛkɛ̃ ɛnawã ye kĩ ɛkɔa Yesu ɔnɔkpa a ɛkpa ye. Ade Pilato kpa osi kĩ bɛfɔnɛ ɔnɔkpa a awɛ bɛkpa ye a.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Foesũ Yosef bɔɛ ɔnɔkpa a, ade ɛkɔa aklala fitaa vɔɛ̃ esuli ye,

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: ɛkɔa ɛnado nɛ ye mɔawɛ linikɛ vɔɛ̃ kĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛnyɛ nɛ de kpale ɔkɛ kamɛ a. Emininya de kpale esua linikɛ a onukpɛ̃ ade ɛdzakũ a.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Maria Magdalani mɔ Maria kĩ a dzi nɛ awã bɛtsɛlamɔ linikɛ a bɛkadã. Badzɔlɛnɛ Na Nɛ Kuni a Ɔwɛ̃.

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Nɛ luwi lɛyade, kĩ lɛnyɛ Kawɛya Luwi ɔnɔnɛ a, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Farisifɔ yatsɛna nɛ Pilato sɛkɛ̃,

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: bibi ye kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, bute anɛ kĩ, kĩ ɔvãlɛvã a na dzi nkpã a, ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Maayidza ndzi nkpã nɛ fuwi ɛlalɛ liti.’

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Foesũ kpa mbla kĩ bɛdzɔlɛ ye kuni a akũ kadzɔlɛ wĩ kɛnaawo luwi lɛlalɛde a akũ. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a aapuli bɛnaaye ɔnɔkpa a lɔkɔa bibi bati kĩ bɛdzɛla ye bitomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ. Ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ ni, liti fɔvã a aaba fɔtɔnyade a.”

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Pilato bi be kĩ, “Mikpã badzɔlɛnɛ minamɛna, midzɔlɛ kuni a akũ ayekĩ míapuli.”

ref: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.
text: Foesũ bɛdzakũ bɛnatim de a nɛ kuni a onukpɛ̃ kɔɔba, ade bɛdzɛla de akũ badzɔlɛnɛ nɛ awã a.

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Nɛ foe kamɛ a, fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-odi a naa Galilea nɛ ɔtɔ a kĩ Yesu tsa be kĩ bɛnaa a akũ.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a tsa fɔlɔ a, bipi adzuni ade bɛkpa badzɔlɛnɛ a sika baũ,

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: Mitsa be kĩ bidi nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ nhɛ mi a akũ. Midã, ndeĩ nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ luwi daa kɛnaawo katinya kawũna.”

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mitavila, mbom minaabi nwaɛ̃ amɛ kĩ bɛnaa Galilea, lɔkɔa baamɔ yĩ nɛ awã.” Badzɔlɛnɛ a Nalɛ Lɛtsa a Kĩ Bɛmɔ́.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Lɛtalɛta tite kuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, kitonɛkĩ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ to ode ɛka ɛyamininya de nɛ linikɛ a onukpɛ̃ a eyi, ɛkɛna akũ edzi nɛ de akũ.

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: Ye akũ flee la ndɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ kamɔanɛ aye, ade ye tɛtadiɛ voe fitaa ndɛ aklala aye a.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Libe wo bikĩ badzɔlɛ fɔkɔ a paa, bɛkatila bliblibli, bɛkɛna ndɛ banɔkpa.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi batsole a kĩ, “Mitavila! Ĩnyina kĩ Yesu a kĩ bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ a miawɛ.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Miwɛ sɔwa minaabi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, ‘Eyidza eto banɔkpa kamɛ. Ɛnyaa mi lɔtɔ yanaa Galilea. Ade awã míamɔ ye a!’ Mite anɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ mbi mi a akũ.”

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Lɛtalɛta Yesu tsɛlamɔ be ade ebi be kĩ, “Lukudɔ litomɔ mi.” Bifuã bɛyapili ye nɛ fɔnɛta, bike aduli bisum ye.

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Kĩ batsole a dzakũ banaa a, kuni a akũ badzɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ badi naa ɔmatɔ kamɛ bɛnabi basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a flee.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: bibi be kĩ, “Milɛ kĩ, ‘Lɛnyɛ a kĩ bɔwa tida a ade ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yaye ɔnɔkpa a bɛdzakũmɛna a.’

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Badzɔlɛnɛ a tɛ sika a, bɛnakɛna ndɛ ayekĩ bibi be a. Foesũ ade fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ lɛgba nɛ Yudafɔ kamɛ bɛnɛɛ balɛ kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ a. Yesu Nyɛ Ye Akũ Ɛtsa Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a.

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Yesu fuã be ɛlɛ kĩ, “Baguma kɔa tite mɔ ode otumi flee edo nɛ yĩ awɛ.

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye a, bike aduli bisum ye, lɛmɛ be kamɛ badi tɛ atsindze.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Nɛ awã naa, bɛfɔnɛ be tɛsau awɛ biyidza bitomɔ ye.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Kĩ bɛnaa lɔtɔ sukuloo a, ɛmɔ bawaɛ̃ nviã Yakobo mɔ Yohanes. Bɛna nɛ be ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ bɛkalɔla be tɛsau.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Foesũ Yesu dĩ gba nɛ Galilea tite akũ flee. Yesu Tsa Bati Beblebee Fɔsɔ.

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: Anɛ kɔkɔɛ a mɔ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya tsɛlɛ eyi kĩ Baguma bi ye obi kĩ, “Maado yĩ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɛnya nɔ lɔtɔ, ɛyaalɔla osi eyi ɛkpa nɔ.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Yesu mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a nawo ɔmatɔ kĩ bavɛ Kapernaum. Nɛ Yudafɔ Kawɛya Luwi a kĩ litomɔ be awã kawo luwi a, Yesu naa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ɛnatsa fɔtsa.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Kĩ Yesu sila ɛnɛ́ eto mui a kamɛ a, ɛmɔ kĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ binya ade Hũhũ a ka eko nɛ ye akũ ndɛ ayekĩ mblɔduma aka a.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Oti odi kĩ yasɔ fɔsɔ ngbã kĩ bati avila ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃. Ɛkɛna akũ ade eke Yesu aduli kĩ ebumɔ ye a. Onyole a bi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ awɛ ni, aapuli ɔfɔ̃ fɔsɔ kĩĩ lɛwɔ nɛ yĩ akũ.”

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: Ɛkalɛ nɛ awã kĩ, “Lɔkɔ wĩ a wo ɔtɔ ade Baguma lɛgãkanya lɔkɔ a fuã. Mimuniki nɛ mi tɔkpa liti lɔkɔa mitɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a mido!”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Kĩ Yesu mɔ be a, ɛvɛ be deedimɔapɛ kĩ bitomɔ ye. Be malo biyidza lɛtalɛta, bɛfɔnɛ be ɔlɛga Zebedeo mɔ ye adzuma bakɛnanɛ a odi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ, ade bitomɔ Yesu a. Yesu Blɔmɛna Hũhũ Kpa Etomɛna Oti Odi Kamɛ.

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Foesũ Yesu wo nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, ade epi ye nɛ kɔkpɔ ɛdzɛla ye kĩ eyidza. Deedimɔapɛ fɔsɔ a dɔ ye, ade eyidza esum be a.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: Kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye a, bibi ye kĩ, “Odi nyɛ odi lamɛna nɔ kawɛ!”

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, onyole odi kĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ na nɛ be katsɛnakɔ̃ a,

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: ade ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Yesu Nazaretni, litsa deĩ nɛ bɔmɔ nɔ kayite? Ɔya kĩ ayaawɔla boe mbɔɛɛ? Ĩnyina oti a kĩ anɛ. Nɔ nyɛ Baguma Oti Kpalɛwa Kĩ Bido a.”

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Yesu wũni nɛ hũhũ a akũ kĩ, “Sua onukpɛ̃, kanɛ nɛ oti a kamɛ.”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Hũhũ a fufulinya oti a mɔ ɔsĩ, ɛfɔ̃ ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ade ɛnɛ nɛ ye kamɛ a.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Fɔkɛna bati a flee kĩ bɛdza nɛ awã saa, ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Litsa nɛ awĩ a? Ei! Fɔtsa katsa vɔɛ̃ ɔdabe nɛ awĩ a? Oti kĩĩ deĩ kɔbɛ̃ yawũni nɛ hũhũ kpa akũ ade yatse ye ɔlɛdo a!”

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Lɛmɛ Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bɔnaa tukula bɛbã kĩ tifuã a lɛmɛ kamɛ. Kɛfɛta kĩ mabi be malo Baguma fɔlɛtsa, kitonɛkĩ ade foesũ ĩya nɛ katinya kamɛ a.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Ye fɔlɛtsa kɔ Yesu kɔnya, foesũ eyo awɛ ɛta ye. Ebi ye kĩ, “Mawɛ lɔmɔ. Nɔ akũ ɛkpalɛ.”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ, Yakobo mɔ Yohanes dzakũ nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, ade bɛnaa Simon mɔ Andrea tɔtɔ kamɛ a.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
